rating 4.0. Great socks to use on airplane but wish it didn't have pattern These are wonderful, they helped my legs not hurt while on the airplane and didn't overheat my legs or feet. If you wear them in hot weather then it course your feet might get sweaty, which happened to me but only after a few hours of having to wear them outside the airport because I didn't have a chance to take them off.

The only thing I'd change is that they have that pattern on them and the seams press in lines on my legs so when going from wearing these for hours and then taking them off to wear shorts and sandals I'm left with indentations of long weird lines in my legs and feet. It looks ugly and they slightly hurt from being pressed in so long. It does bother a bit when you have them on too but not enough to make me feel the need to remove the socks. This is the only reason I'm giving it 4 stars, however they do work really well to help reduce swelling and pain. Just be aware of that in case you're planning on wearing a party dress or something right after that you will have lines in your legs for a bit after taking these off.

rating 5.0. Would highly recommend! One of the best compression socks I've used so far. I also own Physix compression socks for work and I find these make my legs feel so much better. I started developing tiny spider veins on my thighs which I am prone to getting not just because of working at a hospital and being on my feet all day but genetics as well. The spider veins have not gotten worse and my legs don't feel swollen. I also use them for working out when I have deadlift days to protect my shins. Would definitely recommend these to healthcare workers and weightlifters!

rating 4.0. Excellent - Used for Young Kids Disco Birthday Party We purchased these with rather low expectations and were pleasently surprised. We had 20 kids coming to the party and purchsed 3 packages (40 units/pack).

We had a bowl of them near the dance floor and with the lights dimmed and the disco ball going, the kids loved them. Each light has a little elastic strap which fits around the finger. Perfect for kids, a little snug for an adult. At the party the kids put 1-4 of them on each hand and dance away, waving their hands to create fun light effects.

Only about 1 in 20 were defective, which I think is an acceptable rate given how cheap they are. I'd consider these as a disposible, single use toy. If they work longer great, but don't count on it.

rating 1.0. They don't work, and they aren't as described I can't believe I'm going to write such a long review about finger lights, but here goes...

I've purchased finger lights before, and they were everything my kids hoped they'd be: they've had a very long life, the switch is easy to push, and the elastic is made of a comfortable black cloth. These are a whole different story.

First, and most important, most of them didn't work properly. Out of 40 lights, 22 were useless: 6 didn't light up at all, 6 lit up so weakly that you couldn't even tell they were lit, and 10 could light up only if you held onto them and squeezed them hard in some elusive way (different for each one).

Officially, the other 18 lights worked, but 11 of them had switches that were so difficult to operate that I didn't want to give them to a bunch of kids who would just find them frustrating.

That left 7 good finger lights. Out of 40. I guess I can't expect more for something I paid less than $8 for.

Aside from the fact that they don't work, they also don't match the description. First, the picture implies that they have a black cloth elastic. Nope. It's a rubbery colored elastic, which might be fine but looks like it'll split soon enough. I guess I'd be okay with this if the lights actually worked.

Second, they are described as having an "easy push button." It is misleading to describe the on/off switch as a "button." And as I've already said, many of them are very hard to turn on, so "easy" is also a misnomer.

Third, the description says you get "10 packs of 4 lights" and "each package will contain 4 finger lights each with a total of 10 packages you will receive." Nope. I received 40 lights all in a plastic bag, not 10 separate packs with the cardboard "hand" package pictured above. Again, not a big deal if the lights work!

Last, the description also says you get "a total of 10 white finger lights, 10 blue finger lights, 10 red finger lights, and 10 green finger lights." I got 7 white lights, 10 blue lights, 10 red lights, and 13 green lights. Close, but another nope. This definitely wouldn't have been a problem for me--in fact, I probably wouldn't have even counted the colors--but since they don't work and I'm writing this review, I might as well be thorough!

I don't know where to buy good finger lights, but these aren't them!

input: rating 5.0. Looks great.. works great At this price.. Excellent. The side button release is perfect. One note.. If you have a very small wrist. (like I do), the smallest adjustment will just barely work.. I have a small wrist by anybody's standard (all guitar players have small wrists), but it works.... Buy it..

rating 4.0. Solid Watchband Nice quality, but the edges of the band need to be slightly "eased" for which I used #200 wet or dry silicon carbide sandpaper.  It did the trick.  Clasp works well, and it was easy (if you have the right tools) to make the band a bit smaller.  Worth the cost to me.
rating 5.0. Much better than the original band that came with my Citizen ... Much better than the original band that came with my Citizen Eco Watch.  I really like the side push button release.

rating 1.0. Could not make the band shorter to fit my wrist. No room to adjust. I could not make the band shorter to fit my wrist. No room to make a proper adjust. It appears this watch band was designed for men with much bigger wrists than mine. One size does NOT fit all. I would need to take 2 links out of the band to make it fit, and there is no simple or easy way to do that. If you have an average sized wrist, don't waste your money on this watch band. I bought it as an inexpensive replacement for my 20+ year old Tag Huerer, which is still a marvelous watch. Tag Huerer is asking $350 for a similar item. That's quite a premium for a similar watch band with a Tag Huerer logo on the clasp.

rating 5.0. watch and band fit perfect together! The watch I put it on looks very nice, a perfect fit. This was the first time I fit a band to a watch, I had some difficulty. The pins weren't reaching in far enough on one side so I had to bend the prongs that hold the pin in place. This was easy to do it just took a little while for me to figure this out. I imagine this is a common occurrence that I didn't know. That being said it looks like this band was made for this watch!! U cannot tell it is not original. I put it on the SLsport Timex with white face. It comes with a white band that I don't think I would ever where in public. Maybe if I played tennis or I owned a yacht, but unfortunately I do not. The band is not to heavy, the push button clasp is a nice touch, and band was easy to size. All in all a nice purchase that I would recommend! Just wanted to add for the one star reviewer that the band easily adjust smaller by tapping out the link pins like 90 percent of all metal bands excluding the twistoflex type.

rating 3.0. Beautiful band but full of issues The Ritchie SS watchband does look good and the quick release on the clasp seems as if it would be helpful. The problem is that, so far, I cannot install the band on my watch for two reasons.

The first reason I cannot install the band on my watch is that the pin that attaches the band to the clasp is just a straight piece of wire -- it falls out of the band. I am glad that it fell out immediately upon my receipt of it in the mail. Now I know that I cannot trust this band to secure my watch unless I am able somehow to fix this issue. I hope that the band is too large for my wrist requiring removal of one or more of the links and, if so, the pins from the removed links can be used to fasten the band to the clasp.

The second reason I cannot install the band on my watch is that the Ritchie band is shipped without the spring pins that fasten the band to the watch. The pins from my old band will not work as their diameter is larger than the Ritchie band will accept. Hopefully I can purchase suitable spring pins from a jeweler at a reasonable price.

It is a shame that such a nice looking band is fraught with these difficulties. It does make me very concerned on how well the band secures my watch to my wrist.

rating 2.0. don't expect it to look nice on a Seiko SSC017 This watch band is a 20 mm curved end band but, don't expect it to look nice on a Seiko SSC017. The watch case and the curved ends are at two totally different mismatched heights. The spring pins that were supplied were far too long...........I'm thinking they were for a 22 mm watch. It was a real chore to attach it to my watch then, it was not aligned correctly. I ended up opting to order an OEM from totalwatchrepair.com  part# MOES327J9. I not return this band because it will cost almost as much to ship it back. But, I can not say that I recommend purchasing it either.

rating 5.0. A rare find... It isn't often I "rush" to write a review, but this product deserves it! This bracelet is an outstanding value for the money. It fit my watch perfectly, and was easy to install and size, (you will need a friction pin remover). For those of you considering this bracelet for your watch, keep in mind that this is designed to fit a Speedmaster type watch case. It may fit others with some minor modification. Just to be sure, I would suggest keeping the plastic on the strap while fitting it. That way if it doesn't fit you can return it without any problems. The links are solid construction, but overall the bracelet is light weight. The links have a brushed finish on the sides and  back. On the top, the two outer rows of links are brushed as well, with the middle links having nicely polished, bordered edges, (upon closer inspection these polished sections are actually separate links... amazing). This is what I liked about this style of bracelet compared to the typical "oyster" style. The end links, although hollow, are extremely robust and have a fit that I would compare more to a solid end link. They are surprisingly better than most of the factory end links that have come on many of my watches. The clasp is a very basic version of the Speedmaster push button, but seems to to it's job quite well. While this bracelet lacks some of the heft and features you'd expect to find on a "high end" model, it's well worth 3 to 4 times it's price.

rating 4.0. Not for everyone. As was stated in other reviews the shape of the endpoints on the band may not fit every watch perfectly.  With a Dremel tool and some jeweler's pliers, I was able to customize it and it fits fine.  If you are not handy and confident with tools, don't order this.  Go to a jeweler and let them be responsible.  Otherwise, it's a nice, comfortable band.  Easy on, easy off with no pinching.

rating 4.0. Good Value & Secure Watchband I wanted a metal bracelet for an old 23 jewel Bulova wristwatch and this works very well. It was easy to adjust for the proper length, and the locking mechanism works very well: easy to secure and unlatch, but not prone to unwanted release. This has made this watch useful again.

rating 4.0. could be better The band was as expected, solid links, I had one link removed for a proper fit. The only complaint I have is the edges of each link are rather sharp and to make the band perfect the manufacturer should have rounded the edges slightly
rating 5.0. Nice packaging for such an inexpensive product Fits my Samsung Gear S2 classic perfectly. Nice packaging for such an inexpensive product. Tool is very helpful.

rating 4.0. A great bang for your buck I can't complain for the price. It fits my Seiko SRN045P2 for a fraction of the price I would have had to pay for a Seiko equivalent.

I'm knocking it two stars because:
1. It was dirty. There was this black grease scattered throughout the band near the pins, though it was easily cleanable.
2. Lack of attention to detail. If you look at one of the photos below, you'll notice that one of the pins jut out because it's too long.

But I'm giving it a star back because:
1. Included a watch spring bar tool to remove the spring bars.
2. Included two spring bars.

The above would have cost me an extra $10 or so had they not been included. This band is a good bang for the buck.
rating 1.0. One Star Junk, fell apart in 1 month. Just outside of return window.

rating 2.0. It fit my Timex Expedition SS DD watch, but broke after 4 months. Fits my Timex Expedition Stainless indiglo day/date watch perfectly. But I noticed after just a short time it started leaving a red metallic stain on my wrist whenever I sweated or wore it in the rain. (Pretty much every day) I thought this would quit after a few months but so far it hasn't. Then the small stud which secures the lower part of the clasp to the upper part pulled out of the hole in the lower clasp. Since I am not where I can readily replace the band, I cleaned the clasp up and re-installed the stud into the hole using J-B Weld to secure it in place. After allowing sufficient time for the epoxy to cure, the watch was returned to service and is currently undergoing field testing to verify the repair.
Seriously, it is an inexpensive replacement band for a Timex watch. The original band lasted about five years. This one, about 4 months. Next time I will just buy another watch.

rating 4.0. Good quality SS band Good quality SS band.  Just the right about of heft.  Required some grinding with a dremel tool to make the curved ends fit with my Victorinox swiss army watch (officers 1884).

rating 1.0. Broke within six months Didn't last  6 months before it broke. The center stud that locks the clasp together separated from it's pressed in hole rendering the bracelet completely useless. So in the trash it went.

rating 5.0. Looks awesome! So I ordered this band for a different watch (dive watch) but I accidentally ordered the 20mm when I needed the 22mm. So I was going to send it back, then I decided to put it on my jack spade because it fit a 20mm band. This looks awesome! Great band, awesome quality, excellent price!

rating 5.0. Good fit to replace Wenger watch band, Useful tool came as a bonus! My Wenger watch is no longer made ( they went out of business) this item was a perfect fit for my watch! The tool was ideal to remove the old pins and band. The tool was GREAT help to replace the pins. I know I will be using this tool on other watches that me and my wife own. This item came earlier than expected.

rating 5.0. An absolutely great value...if it fits. I originally purchase this Ritche 20mm Stainless Steel Bracelet for my new Victorinox Swiss Army Officer's watch. Unfortunately, it did not fit. I decided to attempt to modify it by bending metal and filing things down, knowing full well that I would not be able to return it I figured, hey the band was only around $10. It was a not going to happen since the spring pin was just too far back and no amount of modification would make those curved end fit. The spring pin hole on this watch almost looked like it was near the top of the lug closer to the face end.
So I decided to try using it on my Wenger SAK watch, which also had a 20mm band. Known to me as "old trusty", I've had that watch for nearly 20 years and still had the original band. I figured that the band could use a face lift, so why not try to use this Ritche band. On the Wenger, the spring pin holes are located at the end of the lug and closer to the back of the watch (as seen in the pic). After bending the curved ends back into shape, I attempted to install the watch band....It fit perfectly fine. My "old trusty's" little quartz heart may give out in about 5 to 10 years, but at least it will look good.when it does go.

Pros:
-Solid links
-Spring pins and spring pin tool included.
-Stylish combination of matte and polished finish as well as very good construction.
-Good value

Cons:
-None really unless it doesn't fit your watch.

As others have said, keep the plastic until you know it fits. If the spring pin holes on your watch look like they're toward the back of the watch and toward the end of the lug, there is a good chance it would fit with no modification (as seen in the pic). If you see that the holes are pretty close to the front of the watch, don't even attempt to mod the curved ends. The scratches on the band are from me trying to mod it, but you have to admit that the band still looks good.

Knowing what I know now about the band, I would buy it and would recommend it to others. It really is an excellent value.

rating 4.0. May work on your watch, initially did not on mine but with a little fitting it did. Band itself looks nice and feels solid; my quibble is with the curved end trim. Placed against my watch they look great, but they push the band so far away from the body of the watch that the pins do not come anywhere near the mounting holes. I'm debating whether to chuck it in the trash or do some surgery with a Dremel tool and grinding stone.I would estimate the holes are a solid millimeter out of line for my watch as is. UPDATE: After very little Dremel work I was able to get this mounted and surprisingly it looks and functions very well. I am upgrading my evaluation to 4 stars accordingly.

For those who ask: Yes, the length is adjustable - the links closest to the latch are pinned in place. You can straighten a large paper clip or use a largish safety pin to push them out; take out both pins at a link and then reattach the remaining link ends with the pin. The pins only want to come out one way.

The manufacture of the watch wanted 50 bucks for a exact replacement Bracelet so this Ritche Bracelet gave my watch additional life at a fraction of the cost.

Picture is the new Bracelet installed next to the old broken Band

rating 5.0. Unique and Lovely I purchased this as an assessory to compliment the Cleopatra costume I am going to wear for Halloween.  I have already worn it without the costume however and it has been a joy.  Please note that the chain around the finger is larger than it seems in the photo.  My hands are quite slim, so the snake falls lower on me than the photograph, but only because the finger chain is too big.  It still looks good the way it fits me however, it is just a little harder to keep it in exact place (not a big deal).  Not everyone will have this problem, I think that the size of your hand matter.  However I do think it is important to keep in mind the large finger chain.  The wrist chain is adjustable and that helps to keep the bracelet snake from moving around.  At any rate, I am happy with my purchase given the price as it looks a more expensive than what I paid for it.  For your information, the snake eyes on my bracelet are black.  Green would have been even better, but I am happy I bought it and I will wear it often.

rating 5.0. i am so happy with this dress I rolled the dice and came out a winner! The material is thicker and stretchier than i could have hoped to expect, which makes for a very forgiving, and flattering in all the right places, dress! The best part is the lace... it truly is lovely and strong and stretchy! Not fragile or tight or constricting at all, (which can tend to happen to girls with bigger shoulders and chests in vintage dresses!) input:
rating 5.0. Great concept, some questions, and observations. My copy of the Winners Bank 200 came with two business cards, a couple of stickers, and a handwritten note by the president of the company, thanking me for my order and even offering me his cell number if I had any questions. That's great customer service.

This gets a 5-star rating, but just barely. The thing that made me vacillate between four stars and five was the fact that nowhere in the packaging, the personal note, or even stuffed in the envelope were there any instructions on how to use it. Fortunately I knew that I had seen a YouTube video about the Winners Bank 200, and it was there that I discovered the instructions to folding one's bills correctly so that they could fit into the bank. Considering another review on here said that they received instructions, I found it perplexing that they weren't printed either on the box, or even slipped into the note that I had received.

That aside, I used the Winners Bank 200 last night when I went to the casino. Here are my honest observations:

You have to be fairly adept at folding the bills nice and tight. If the bills you have are crumpled or bent, that can affect your ability to fold them neatly/tightly enough to fit into the Winners Bank. Unfortunately, that was the case for me at the casino, but I did the best that I could and the plunger did help, although as the night wore on, just having four or five bills in the bank caused a real difficulty for the plunger to go back in. Maybe there's some trick to manipulating the plunger as it goes back into the bank that forces the bills to the front or to the back, but that wasn't detailed in the video, nor could I seem to figure it out.

While I understand the necessity for keeping the opening in the winners Bank narrow, it seemed to be a lot narrower than in the video. Since the bank is also supposed to hold casino chips, I was perplexed, because I don't think I could have gotten a single chip through the opening on my bank. I know that there are some small pieces of foam inside the bank by the aperture; what I don't know is whether those are supposed to be removed or not?

For someone who doesn't necessarily win huge amounts, there bears the question of when you cash out your tickets, do you wait to consolidate your money long enough to get a larger denomination bill, or do you fill the bank with the existing bills you have on you, knowing that you will eventually run out of room? Maybe this means once the bank gets too full, you go home, or go back to your hotel room and empty the bank and go back out on the floor? (But ah, the temptation to put some of that banked money back into your bank roll when you leave the room again...).

You also need to find a spot at the casino where you can not only set down the bank for a moment, but that has ample room for you to fold your bills and the bills that haven't yet been folded. This of course has nothing to do with the bank itself, but it was pointed out to me by another casino goer that you tend to have your hands full juggling the bank, your bills, and then trying to fold things. On a crowded casino floor, you may not have such space, and you may have to take yourself too a bar or some other off-floor area.

My criticisms aside, this bank is great. I took $100 with me last night and banked the overage everytime I cashed out a ticket. There was genuine delight, and let's be honest, relief, that when I came home and unlocked the bank, I did indeed come home with money. :) For that reason alone, I recommend this product.
rating 5.0. It works! Really nice!  Used it the the day I got it.  Wasn't disappointed, it paid for itself the first time out.  I would recommend it to anyone who likes going to the casino.
rating 5.0. Look no further. Winners Bank 200 is all you'll ... Look no further.  Winners Bank 200 is all you'll need.

rating 5.0. Leave the casino a Winner! This is a first class build in a personal safe. The smoothly engineered lines are rugged and pleasing. It's so obviously superior to the heavier knockoff brand you may have seen. This is the real deal and the right choice. Where were these when I was a young guy in the Navy? We made regular Atlantic City runs back in the mid-eighties. I would have brought home so much... The degree of behavioral psychology employed by our beloved gaming institutions ensures that many people, including myself, will willingly fall in love with the dream of improving our situations so desperately that our common sense becomes compromised. The Winner's Bank200™ is so perfectly matched to these impulse control issues for us at the casino. Put some cash in your safe and leave your key at home. The next time you are gaming you will actually have fun and leave happy for once! I love the product build, design, and lifetime warranty. Thanks again, Jun! I'm available for public relations in Maine anytime!

rating 5.0. It works!!!! I leave the key ... It works!!!!  I leave the key at home, put winnings in my winners box and take my winnings home.  I don’t have access to winnings even if and tempted to play it back.

rating 5.0. Perfect Gift Purchased this for my boyfriend and I think he really liked having the challenge of putting his winnings away instead of playing them back. Perfect gift for your gambling friends.

rating 5.0. I LOVE IT!!! Ok, so this was recommended by a lady I met while I was on a promotion’s paid trip to Atlantic City. I was excited because there have been times I have left the casino with NOTHING. It doesn’t happen all the time but the times it does happen really makes me unhappy. So I definitely wanted to give this a try. It was packaged very well. The product itself is nicely made. Durable and has a little weight to it. It’s the perfect size to keep in your purse. Very discreet. I tried it out for the very first time this weekend. I had a strategy in mind that I would initially bank whatever I made on my free play. I had about $300 in free play that I played first. I banked pretty much $100% of my winnings from the free play  (it wasn’t a whole lot but hey it was something). The $700 in cash I played with throughout the night. I wasn’t real strict with that money. I put in the bank what I felt I wouldn’t miss. Honestly I just didn’t want to put in too much and then panic or become anxious once I ran out of money. So as the night (early morning ended) I reached the end of my $700. I wasn’t sure how much I put in total. I wouldn’t find that out until I got home where I left the key. So with no more money to gamble with, I got me some food and went up to my room. The next morning I did feel like I wanted to gamble some more but all my money was locked up. It did bother me a little but I had to remind myself of the whole purpose. Usually I would play a little before leaving (losing more money), but since this wasn’t an option I headed home. I couldn’t wait to get home so I could see what I banked. The last two pics shows my winnings. I came home with $500! I was thinking I only saved about $350. What a pleasant surprise! I do feel I could have saved more, but I’m still happy. I will refine my strategy on my next visit. Overall a must if you’re SERIOUS about leaving the casino with something...but you have to LEAVE KEY AT HOME! Otherwise what’s the point? You would be setting yourself up for failure.

I'm afraid that when it does get caught, piece will go flying off and break. Other than that, its not too heavy, its pretty, and its about the size of a palm.

rating 4.0. Reliable & cheap watch Well it's a $10 watch so you can't expect a whole lot out of it. I had one of these years ago and replaced the band about five times. The thin watch band will break eventually if you wear it a lot. Had it for about 14 years and still worked when I threw it away. The hole for the spring bar was worn and the band would slip off easy so that is why it went to the trash. This watch is well worth $10. The light on it is very poor. If you are looking for a reliable and cheap beater watch then this is it. Back when I had this watch they were made in Japan. Now made in Thailand.

rating 5.0. Classy, in a $10 sorta way The Casio Fox 91 Whiskey (F91W) is a classic that makes a statement: "You don't have a whole lot of money, but you do have a whole lot of class."
The F91W is extremely easy to operate, even for those who can't program the time on a VCR.
It's versatile. One time zone. An alarm. Stop watch with laps. It does it all!
Water resistance? You bet your bottom dollar! Swim with it. Shower with it. Pretty soon the F91W will be your new best friend!

The F91W will always keep you on time. In one time zone.

rating 4.0. Only problem is the strap. Roughly every three to four years the strap on this watch breaks and it is far cheaper to buy the watch again than a strap (yes, I tried several times). This watch arrived earlier than promised and before the strap on my old watch finished giving way. This watch was twice the price of the last one (exact same model). The strap feels a little stiff so I wonder if it has been sitting on a shelf for years. But it functions perfectly and has all the features I need. My one gripe is the light is too dim to read the watch.

rating 5.0. Captured by crazy military, Watch keeps Ticking even when my heart stops. As a middle eastern hipster it's easy for me to find old 80's American and Japanese technology at the local bazar. I spied this gem for a pittance of a chicken and HAD to have it. It was better then finding a fixie hauling PBR!! (If I were allowed to drink) No sooner had I strapped my retro classic to my wrist and checked the time that a black hood closed over my head. At first I hoped it was irony but I was wrong SO WRONG!

I was soon moving in a truck, I could feel sweat beading under the rubber strap of my Casio F91W-1 and knew the timepiece was still attached to my wrist able to tell me how long I'd traveled. Alas I awoke in a haze, as my hands clenched behind me I could not check the time.
I couldn't distinguish the pain of the zip tie cuffs from my Casio F91W-1 smooth rubber strap but soon found my new watch dangling before my face as a man screamed "WHY DO YOU OWN THIS WATCH!?!" "To know what time it is I muttered meekly," I could see it was 1AM, I was missing Adventure Time being broadcast from the United States. "CAN YOU TELL ME WHY 32 OTHER TERRORISTS HERE HAVE THIS SAME WATCH!?!" I searched my brain, 32? That sales man said I had the only one in the country, I wasn't unique anymore? I was going to need a new watch. "They were all lied to by the salesman as well?" I answered still thinking I was being persecuted as a hipster yet again *le sigh. "A wise guy eh? We have ways of making you talk"

I was reluctantly given my watch back, I was told it was so I'd know which way to pray with my Casio F91W-1's compass feature but the man didn't believe that I wasn't religious but that my outfit was indeed chosen for irony. Seriously, robes in a desert, how do you not get that?

I soon heard through the grape vine that I've be transferred to Cuba, Knowing little of the world from my antique text books I thought Cuba was still the Monte Carlo of the American corrupt and looked forward to my pleasant tropical extravaganza.

What I underwent in Cuba was more horrible then `The Situation' trying to roast a Hummer H2 with Rebecca Black Singing Friday in the Front seat (or back) but despite years of agony and much water exposure my Casio F91W-1 still works keeping the time of my innocence alive.

rating 1.0. No longer 5 star I have another Casio, G-Shock G7700, I bought this F-91W for daily use when I don't need G-shock for sport. F-91W's not applicable for sport, but it's just enough.

The G7700 is a little too big for daily use, and I have to take it off when I sleep for its shape make me uncomfortable. The biggest problem is the strap keep accumulating nasty white thing in its gap and web pattern, I need to clean it really often. Design of it is awful, they didn't consider how it really feel. Therefore, I chose this one.

For F-91W:

Pros:
1. Light weight, 20g vs 52g of G-shock, I can't feel it on my wrist.
2. The strap is easy to clean and smooth.
3. Cost less, it covered 99% percent of daily time use. I don't think many people need a world time and an 1/1000 stopwatch.

Cons:
1. Only three operation button, and one blank slot.
2. If I can be 2-3mm bigger, it would be perfect.

Overall, it's a good watch for the price and normal casual occasions.

rating 5.0. All function with just enough form to have become an enduring, timeless classic timepiece For the right kind of person, with just exactly the right sort of style (or appropriate lack thereof), the Casio F91W is every bit as good as, if not better than, a Cartier. Th F91W is a classic. It's remained virtually unchanged for decades, and there's a reason for that. Certainly, it has its own look. It's slim, sleek, and sports the cleanest of clean lines. It's simple, and it doesn't make a big fuss. It's comfortable, it's accurate, and it does enough. Its alarm is positively reliable, and its stopwatch is simple and consistent. It's small and unassuming, and doesn't draw a lot of attention to itself because it doesn't need to. This watch exists for its wearer and no one else. It doesn't try to impress, but it will never let you down. It does a few things and it does them well, all the while, not pretending to care at all about the things it doesn't do.

It's a perfect watch for a woman or a man, for a child or an adult. It's good for sport, and it's good for relaxing, and it's good for work, and it's just fine for everything else in between.

If this watch were a person, when asked the time, it would reply "quarter to three!" with a big grin on its face, and go back to its drink without missing a beat. It wouldn't huff and fumble for the time and act as if you were intruding on it before somehow giving you a time that was an hour too fast or slow.

But you knew that already, didn't you? It used to be that you could find an F91W accidentally, just out shopping in the department stores. And maybe you'd need a new watch so you'd pick one up. But today, and especially online, the F91W doesn't really find you as much as you go looking for it. You wouldn't be looking at it if you didn't already know it was perfect for you. And if it's not the watch for you, that's fine too, just move along to something else. The F91W isn't bothered. It knows who its people are.

rating 5.0. Great Wach: Lets settle the "Water Resist" once and for all Now then, this is a fine digital watch, but there seems to be much confusion as to what the "Water Resist" term means on the face. Simply put, "How wet can I safely get my Casio F-91W?" Now then ISO standard 2281 define what makes a water resist watch.[...]
From this text I will quote section 6.2.1
"6.2.1 Resistance to air overpressure

Subject the watch to an air overpressure of 2 bar[2] and measure the flow rate of air entering the case."

Hence we can conclude that any watch bearing the marking "Water Resist", can at minimum survive at a pressure of 2bar, which in water is about 10m. Further reading in section 7.2 implies that could be closer to 20m in water, as air is more permeable. So Casio owners feel safe, you can dive to the bottom of your local pool and the F-91w should remain intact.

rating 5.0. Great affordable versatile watch. Cult classic and justly so. If you've never tried one, you will likely be pleasantly surprised. Of course it looks exactly like what it is, a cheap watch; but a good one. Try not to bend the strap back over the buckle like you would with a leather strap and it will last a lot longer. I have and enjoy other more expensive watches from Citizen, Seiko, Rotary; this Casio gets a lot of wear time. When the strap eventually does break, a NATO type seems to be the popular choice for a replacement. Very long-wearing. Read some reviews for this watch. I learned a thing or two.

Cons: Sometimes the buttons can be inadvertently pushed. Visibility in some lighting conditions may be lacking. If you really snag the strap it can break or tear. If the little keeper breaks the strap may soon break too, right at the buckle. The light is a bit weak.

Pros: Light as a feather. Adjustable to a wide range of wrist sizes, so a fun choice for a child's watch. Many colours to pick from. Digital display, if you've never had one, is instant and positive. For some people, reading an analog face can be confusing, especially when one is used to am / pm, and confronted with 24 hour times; when you are traveling and are tired, for example. So the ability to swap the display between am / pm or 24 hours is a plus. The alarm is useful. Materials are more unaffected by sweat, saltwater, and grime than a leather strap. So, useful as a work watch, or for many outdoors activities. Very inexpensive, almost disposable. Widely available, so easily replaced. Long battery life. Operation of buttons and features is easy.

Very highly recommended!

rating 5.0. Just the right touch. These earrings have proven to go with so many different outfits!  The barest hint of the stylish rose gold base is evidenced through the white pearlized enamel overlay.  The five teensy colorful stones add just the right touch of sparkle.  The earrings are large enough to be noticed, without overpowering your outfit, and have just enough sheen and color to pump up what you are wearing.  I can wear these to work of for a lively dinner date. These earrings have become my alternative to pearls, my I-don't-know-which-earrings-to-wear choice.  I wear, and enjoy, them once or twice each week.  I feel that they were a wise purchase.  (I also purchased the matching ring.)

rating 5.0. Beautiful This is really a beautiful ring. It shines like real diamonds, is comfortable on.
I ordered this purely out of love for eternity bands, but not being able to afford a real diamond one.
Delivery was quick. I'm so happy for ordering this ring & absolutely love it!
It goes well with my other rings, I stacked it with a heart shaped diamond ring, no one can tell this is not real.
Incredible value, amazing beautiful ring. This is definitely a great ring.

rating 5.0. Very nice ring This ring really does look like it costs more & came from a jeweler. The stones shine so well, they look real.
One note is the color of titanium is not like silver, it is more of a darker metal, almost looks black.
Either way, I wanted this for my husband because he works with his hands & the original wedding band I purchased from a jeweler was getting bent out of shape & scratched very badly.
Upon first seeing this one, we are both impressed. It looks great & is a thick band. My husband says this one is more comfortable than the other one!
We are very happy with this purchase & recommend this ring to anyone who is looking for something stylish, affordable, that really does look like you spent a whole lot more on it.
This is definitely a nice ring.

rating 4.0. I couldn't help myself... Okay, here's a breakdown review of dimension of this crazy nice shirt. Like all the other reviewers said, the fabric quality is really high. It's made well with good stitching and secure buttons. The button pockets are a lie though. There is a pocket and a flap with a button sown into it. So a pocket.

It feels like real cotton flannel fabric - except mine is marked 65% polyester and 35% viscose. I only mention that because the product description says that it's made with 100% cotton. Not a big deal. Warm enough for fall and early spring on its own. The fabric is stiff enough to be able to pop a collar. Don't do that. You're better than that.

I brought a Chris Green 4x shirt. Dimensions are exactly that given on the product page. The length given is from neck to bottom of the shirt. There's rise in the hip sides, so they're about 2 inches shorter. It was nice and roomy and fell at just the right length to be a shirt dress. Wear tights; it's not long enough for that nonsense.

There are sleeve ties for you to roll your sleeves up, but the sleeves are just a bit too tight for me to completely roll them up correctly. The fabric is stiff enough to stay folded once though, so all is good. There are ties on the bottom of the shirt in case you want to tuck it up to normal shirt length.

If you like this shirt in exactly those dimensions, then wash in cool water and line dry.

If you're a curious rebel like me, then wash in warm water and tumble dry warm. It shrank. Duh.

Lable Size 4XL (WASHED): Length 28.5"/ Chest 37.5"/ Shoulder 17.1"/ Sleeve 22.5"

It's now a slightly boxy shouldered,3/4 sleeve shirt. Oddly, the shoulders did not shrink at all. And there's no dress about it. It will not completely cover your butt. Do not try that. Not even with tights.

I like it. It's still comfortable, but forget about using any of the ties. Wear this, a pair of jeans and boots to fulfill whatever country girl whim you like.

I'm buying another one of these, but now I'm curious about the 6x....

rating 1.0. SIZES ARE MUCH SMALLER THAN WHAT THEY CLAIM. Sizes run WAY too small. I ordered the 6XL expecting it to be a 3XL (even a small 3XL should have fit me, and it was WAAAY TO SMALL. Actual size was probably a Large. Don't trust these sizes they're way more off than what they claim.

rating 5.0. LOVE LOVE LOVE this shirt!! I had to order the 6X in order for it to be an XL in US sizes and that fit me perfect! Right out of the box it looked awful, but once I put it on, it tapers in at the waist and is very comfy! I wear an XL in tops and 38DD so I always worry with the fit of button up shirts, but this buttoned easily!

Update: I have now purchased this shirt in another color, and wore it today with the sides buttoned up which I think helps to give me a slimmer look. I have attached pictures. With a necklace and some ankle boots, this shirt can be dressy for going out to a nice dinner, or you can throw on some converse with regular jeans and be casual.

Unfortunately only 3 out of the 5 shirts I ordered came with had the button holes sewn up but I'm just going to fix them myself. Worth the price for $11. They do feel like good material and aren't thin and flimsy like most women's flannel shirts. The buttons on the pockets on the chest are just sewn on the top flaps, so just for show.

rating 2.0. Doesn't Fit... First negative review I've written. I ordered two of these shirts after spending a long time reading reviews and looking into the sizing. I'm a dress size 12/14 so I ordered a 6XL (US 14) and it doesn't button at all. I have a DD chest but they fit into all my other clothes. But not these shirts. Also not anywhere close to being tunic length as shown in multiple reviewer photos. They barely cover my butt when unbuttoned. The color is saturated and true to the pictures so that's a plus. And the outside is a soft flannel. But the inside has a stiffer, scratchier hand to it. Hoping it softens up when washed. For the price, I'll keep to just wear as a light jacket or throw over a tank top. But I'd been hoping to wear with leggings and a wide belt as a tunic and that clearly won't happen. Disappointed...

Owell my loss I got ripped. Buyers beware. I would add a picture of the box but I don't know how. The box has the same as seen on TV logo but if you read the logo says similar style seen on TV made in china. I contacted the seller they told me I could return them. I emailed them back asking if they was going to pay the shipping to return them. They said no. it cost me 20.00 for 2 pair with shipping. It will cost me 8.00 to ship them back so in the end I will get a 10.00 refund in the end I am out a total of 18.00 bucks. Another example of china ripping the American people off. rating 5.0. Use your head, it's a WATCH I bought two, one for a friend. I've collected watches since 1963, upwards of 100 in the "high end" genre and lately I've been buying the  "cheap" variery, simply because I like it. One thing I've learned about Asian watches, with a ltttle attention, these pieces are accurate and look good, IF one follows a simple rule of thumb...handle them like EXPENSIVE watches. I wouldn't simply toss a Rolex into a drawer, then after a few days, complain that Rolex sold me a rag! When I unpacked this Movado knock-off, I followed my own philosophy. Truth be known, one had popped off half the band. It wasn't broken (as many may think), it just needed to have a pin (spring bar) slipped back in place. BTW, this a really good copy. Anyway, know that I bought one of these about 6 month ago and, with a leather replacement band, and there isn't anything wrong with it except it lost about 2-3 minutes a month  (as do ALL quartz!). My partner loves his new "Movado" & I have a back up for whatever.

input: rating 4.0. Very interesting piece So was definitely drawn to this look. The picture looks like a coat like material shirt.  I got the XL as I know alot of these designs will run small. So it arrived faster then I expected since the XL was not prime. It reminds me of those sheets you would find at walmart in the complete bed in a bag. It resembles that kind of noisy cheap sheet. Or like a vinyl shower curtain. The cuffs and buttons are a hollow type of plastic. So I was like nope. Tried it on and it was surprisingly cute and nicely shaped. It looked like the picture minus the fabric. However I could not come close to buttoning it. It would not come pass half my chest. I was bummed as it was so interestingly cute and had a nice style. The sleeve length was great and the full length in back was perfect. So I had my daughter model it. She is 5'6 115lbs. This is not for plus size. My bust is 38/44. I think the XL would be a junior medium/small large. Unfortunately they only go to a XL. If they had a 3X it would probably fit much better. I would actually reorder if they had a bigger size lol. Call me crazy but like I said it was definitely a different type of cute. I liked how it fit everywhere else. I think its worth a try. It reminds me of something you would get from wish.

rating 1.0. Never bought something so colossally small. This was WAYYYYY too small.  Like I couldn’t even move my arms in it.  I couldn’t get the two sides to cover 1-2 thirds of my chest.  I ordered an XL and it felt like a 4.  It’s really a shame cause it looked like it would have been really cute too.  I almost think I was sent the wrong thing.  I admit I’m like a 12-14 in a shirt, and part of me felt that it might be a little snug, but this was just bizarre - comical, even!  The one thing I can say was done objectively well about this top was how strong the seams were.  Then again, they’d have to be, I suppose, in order to survive a lifetime of confused customers yanking the hell out of it, wondering why it won’t even make it around their body.  Would probably make an excellent straight jacket... then again, I’ve seen people with more mobility in one of those than this button down shirt.

rating 4.0. happy with purchase !st - I wear sizes from 12 - 16 and I don't like tight. I ordered the xxxl (16) and they fit how I wanted them to. They are roomy and don't "stick" to me anywhere, allowing room room for my choice of top underneath. These are stretchy and comfortable - I wore them to work which requires a lot of movement and squatting - no problems at all. The pockets are nice and fit essentials. I will be ordering more after a few weeks (I just want to be sure the seems are good- but I see no indication otherwise). They, like so many other pieces of clothing could be considered to be seen through in the right light - but they re not what I consider 'thin' material

And after trying them on, they fit almost perfectly! I usually wear size 16/18 and my hips are way bigger then my waist so I often times have to much room in the waist and not enough room in the hips but these were perfect! They were a bit big in the waist but not as bad as I expected! Overal they are great for the price and are super comfy! However, just so you know they are a little thicker material which is great for the type of weather I will be in.. but maybe not so great if you are going to the Bahamas! Just a heads up. But other wise......AWESOME!

rating 3.0. Good product, shipping is far too expensive I got these for work at a summer camp that doesn't want counselors to have visible piercings. They work well for the purpose as the clear plastic just blends in with my skin, but I wish these came with some sort of fastener as it's very easy to lose one and not notice. One major strike is that the shipping is ridiculously high, ludicrously more expensive than the product. I cannot believe they actually cost that much to ship, and the shipping cost should not be doubled when ordering an extra set. It's not like two of these tiny plastic parts weigh that much more than one does, and you'd have to order them by the hundreds to make up even a pound of shipping weight. So why are you charging so much for mailing???

rating 5.0. This saved me I've had my nose pierced for only a little over three months. I had some major problems with my nose piercing for awhile. I went to the piercing shop where I got it pierced at. It was because the quality of jewelry i had in it was nickel. I put that in since my first one fell out and I couldn't find it. The people at the piercing shop changed it to this one that I ordered. I've had this one in for over a month and I recommend it! I have to wear this for school (even though I shouldn't be even wearing a retainer). It's not noticeable at all!! My friends always ask if I took it out, but I actually still have it in. It's that invisible to people! You don't need to put makeup over it or anything. Just put the stud in and you're done!

rating 5.0. SURPRISED! Legit high rise for my tall ladies/fellas! Ok, I am on board with the majority of folks who ordered these leggings. I was seriously surprised at how much I like these. At 5'9, high rise means something a little different to me as opposed to my 5'4 sisters and brothers out there, but these are legit high rise! I typically wear compression leggings--these are not compression leggings. They are very soft, but the waist on up feels secure and doesnt move around much which is a nice surprise.

As with any pair of leggings, lighter colors are definitely going to show sweat in the crotch area, these are no exception. They are soft, legit high waisted, great colors, stay put for the most part and a great price. I havent washed them yet, so I may update this, but so far so good.

rating 4.0. Size down Good stretchy workout leggings! Did come with small hole in left thigh, was a bit large, kept having to pull them up during leg day workout. Was baggier on me especially in the crotch area, I weigh 168 ordered a large and needed a medium. But good material, not see through and fits and flatter any figure! Would buy again in smaller size!

rating 5.0. Comfort and quality leggings, for a reasonable price! Comfort and style exactly as pictured, even for a bigger girl. The stretchiness is perfectly matched with quality material. I knew these were going to be a tad tight for me, I’m postpartum and know that the belly will go down (hence the exercise clothes!) :) Sometimes the cheaper leggings won’t feel as sturdy or hold up in the wash, but these feel like they will last a long time!
I read so many reviews about these pants and knew I had to check them out! The customer service is excellent as well, the owner emailed back and forth with me to ensure I picked a product that would fit my body the best. I’m very impressed and will shop with Auxjox again as my pants size changes! Can’t wait to see what colors they come up with!

rating 5.0. Great customer service UPDATE:
So my feelings about these completely changed after the companies customer service got ahold of me and offered to send a new pair. Not only did the lady from customer service offer to send me a replacement of the exact same color, but also offered to send me a different color as well at no extra cost. The new leggings I received are amazing and super comfortable. However, I do still wish they had a larger amount of sizing (the only reason I didn’t rate these at 100%).
ORIGINAL REVIEW:
I was so so excited to get these leggings, it seemed like everyone was raving about them. However, when I received them I immediately tried them on and loved the way they felt but as soon as I did a squat to see if they were squat proof, not only could I see my underwater through the material, but the seem immediately pulled apart almost ripping. I’m not sure if I received a defective pair or maybe should’ve gotten a different color but I’m so so disappointed:(

I mean it wasn’t awful, but I stopped at less than 2 miles and I usually go 3.5 because I didn’t want it to get worse. More like a darker tinge on the crotch sweat regions. I also bought the shorts. input: rating 4.0. They had gone from shiny to looking almost like brushed nickel and I was an unhappy camper I bought this polishing cloth to clean my silver colored (but brass I believe?) Alex and Ani bracelets that I wore to a music festival and got very dusty and sealed on with what I assume was my aerosol sunscreen. They had gone from shiny to looking almost like brushed nickel and I was an unhappy camper. Sure, it took some elbow grease and yes it made the cloth disgusting (and I wonder where all the black came from since they didn't look like they had soot on them), but they worked very well! Their shine and brilliance is returned and I don't have to replace them. I assume I can use these on many different items of jewelry and I rate it as a GOOD BUY, especially for the price.
rating 5.0. Works great This is just like an old cloth I have from years ago but is "full" from so much polishing.  This is the next generation and does an excellent job.

rating 5.0. Very Effective Easily removes tarnish and restores shine and luster to jewelry, particularly costume.  I've used it on several pieces of jewelry, and have found it to be very effective.  The dual sided cleaning cloth is particularly appealing, since one side cleans and the other polishes.  Highly recommended.
rating 2.0. Doesn't do the job. It barely cleans silver after much effort and it was absolutely necessary to use silver polish to complete the job.
rating 5.0. Five Stars Exactly what I was looking for.

rating 5.0. Polishes and cleans metals as promised I was slightly surprised to discover that the cleaning cloth (yellow one) and the polishing cloth (blue one) were sewn together. After using the two it now seems to make sense. The yellow cloth cleans and appears to remove minor scratches at the same time, but that might just be a function of the cleaning process. The yellow cloth will get dirty (black smudges) but it will continue to work regardless. The blue cloth removes the cleaning agent and shines the object to a high gloss. Instructions indicate no need to wash the two cloths at all and I now see why not. Those smudges that appear will continue to clean and the blue one will polish until the holes are too big to work further. I suggest keeping the cloth in its original bag or a large baggie that seals so that it maintains the agent in the cloth and to keep it from just becoming another old cloth around the house.
Be careful with certain soft stones and gems (like opals) but if you pay attention to that you will be satisfied.
[[ASIN:B000FIS5U4 Polishing Cloth for Silver, Gold, Brass & Most other Metals, 12"x15" Largest Size]]

rating 4.0. Nice Product.  Wish finishing cloth wasnt sewed together When polishing small objects (like rings & Jewelry) this cloth is just way to big.  The finishing polishing cloth is a nice bonus that I didnt even realize was included.  It would be nice if it wasn't sewn into the  larger blue cloth.  Not a big deal.  I simply cut the blue cloth in half and cut the stitching out and separated the 2.  I have noticed that the blue cloth does leave some lint (easily removable) when polishing rough edges.  Overall I would give this a recommended.  It works as advertised.  I would imagine there may be more suitable sized cloths available.

rating 5.0. I wasn't convinced by the reviews Sometimes, when I look up a product online and the reviews are all raving about it, I figure that the reviews are mostly from people who work for the company or something like that. Honestly, this product had that ring to me - the reviews were all so positive, I thought for sure they were all fake. I bought it anyway because I'm very comfortable with Amazon customer service & return policy.

I had some jewelry that needed polishing. Some is sterling silver dipped in rhodium, some is gold. I didn't want to buy many different cleaners and thought that this might have a chance to be a one stop shop.

The day it came, I pulled it out and laughed - it looked like just a regular microfiber dust rag. Then I used it. In a matter of 10 seconds, the ring I used it on was shiny again. Wow. I was really excited by that. Then I tried it on a few other pieces - all were sparkling in a matter of seconds. I was seriously amazed.

So. Whenever I get really excited about a product, I think about all the other ways that I might be able to use it. Then it hit me. Last week was my anniversary with my husband and we drank a glass of champagne out of the toasting flutes from our wedding to celebrate. My husband washed them afterward but didn't dry them... The stems are made from silver. When I woke up in the morning, I found our toasting flutes to be horrifying - I actually cried. I thought they were ruined forever because the silver was all yellowed and horrible.

This morning, I got the idea to try this rag out on the stems. I kid you not, in about 2 minutes of scrubbing with the rag (they were horrible), my toasting flutes looked almost brand new again. There are a few spots that probably wont be able to be fixed because it seems like the silver kind of got almost burnt off.. but seriously, the glasses are silver again and they're beautiful. The rag on the other hand.. it's totally black from this big project. But for only a few dollars, I'll happily buy another one to use on my jewely. For only a few dollars, this cloth saved my irreplacable toasting flutes.

If you're looking for something to polish your jewelry, this is a great, perfect product - I'll be using it forever. If you're now thinking about all the other things you can use it to polish, I do suggest buying a few clothes at the same time because big projects ruin it kind of fast. However, it was worth every single penny. I couldn't be happier.

rating 3.0. Just so so This mask flops down and keeps needing to be adjusted. The nose wire is not strong enough and it’s too short to hold where you put it. It’s easier to breathe, but that’s due to the design. I think I’d look around a little more if I were to do it again.

rating 4.0. Singer's masks We can't all wait to get out of masks.
As a member of church choir, it is nice to have a mask which isn't getting pulled into your mouth, and allows better air flow. This one does. Still get a little steaming of my glasses with this mask.

rating 1.0. Too thin to work well This mask is extremely sheer. I wished it was made out of thicker fabric. The fabric used in this mask is thinner than any regular mask I have purchased from target. I just wouldn’t feel safe using this mask. To anyone out there, I would just suggest purchasing the Broadway Relief mask that this one is based off of. It is thicker and sturdier AND it supports Broadway actors without work (also only $23).
rating 5.0. Huge help during Covid when I need to sing in person and need to wear a mask. Definitely a must for vocalists and speakers

rating 5.0. Perfect Masks for Performers These masks are great for performers! We are using them in acting styles class as well as for our vocal rehearsals for our school musical. The masks wash and dry perfectly, and the students love having them.

rating 5.0. Very breathable Allows me to breath easily, especially when I have to wear for 3 hours or more

Design make obstruct some of you view downward but for me was a perfect trade off

rating 5.0. It works well. I am part of an adult choir and I use this mask during practice and performance. I've watched recordings of the performances and can pick my voice out clearly, no obstruction. It is comfortable to wear and use. A little hot under the lights but that is to be expected.

rating 5.0. Singing is a Breeze I have worn different masks when trying to sing and this mask is the best that I have found.  I can breathe easily in this mask and be able to breathe only when noted to.... in other masks I have run out of breath before making it to the next rest.... I would not wear this mask other than when singing as I want MORE PROTECTION when I am not singing... But, in our socially distanced choir recording sessions, it really helps me to be able to sing well....

rating 5.0. Must-have for a singer This is a great mask for singing or speaking...possibly even exercising - anything where you have to draw a deep breath! The fabric is held far enough away from your mouth that you do not suck it into your mouth when inhaling deeply. You can fully open your mouth without the mask slipping or moving. The wire over the nose is really snug fitting. This is the first mask I've been able to wear with my glasses and not fog up, even with deep breathing. The ear straps are adjustable as well. Wish I had found it sooner!! The whole mask is 2 layers of fabric. My only critique is that 3 layers on the front panel may have made it even better.

rating 5.0. Best singing mask solution around The material is light weight and the mask looks natural and normal on your face.I have used the mask for a few weeks now for choir type singing and the sound volume is great.Breathing is much easier.The only thing that could be improved is that the bottom part gets a little to close to your vocal cords and is at times a little uncomfortable.But not a big problem, I just folded it in a little.May be they should consider making male/female sizes.

rating 5.0. Game changer for musicians during COVID As a public school chorus teacher who wears glasses, this mask is a game changer. Not only does the mask not move on your face when talking, it has no issue when singing tall vowels for long periods of time as well. Because of the breathable fabric, it fogs up glasses a lot less than normal masks when worn correctly and the straps are adjustable to acquire this much more easily. Although it looks like a platypus’ bill, I take comfort over vanity any time when it comes to masks as most everyone has to wear them right now.
rating 5.0. A great mask I used these while teaching in person this spring. They allowed me a full range of voices and volumes without falling off my face or slipping out of place.

rating 5.0. Singing is easier! I sing in a church choir, and found it difficult with a regular mask. This singer’s mask gives my mouth and my chin much more freedom to move and articulate the words. It doesn’t even allow my glasses to fog up when I am singing!

rating 1.0. too thin This has so many positive reviews, but . . . .  First time I wore this, while in a store, I was impressed with how comfortable is.  Then I looked closer.  The material directly in front of the mouth is reasonably thick, but top and bottom are too thin to make any difference... you can easily see through it.  No wonder it's so easy to breathe through!  I wish I could send it back, I'm going to have to go back to wearing double disposables (which I wash and reuse) and try a different singers mask. Very disappointed

rating 1.0. Way over priced This mask is way over priced. It is very shallow and not much better then a regular mask. rating 2.0. Not as advertised! This is a flimsy excuse for a singers mask. Do to the fact that I need it as a teacher and the other masks out there don't work well for me, I might try to modify it, but will probably return it. Key problems:

2) It has no structure; no wire or anything to hold it away from your face.
3) It STINKS! Smells like plastic from a factory when you put it on.

rating 2.0. Beware - not made of cotton I was optimistic about this mask for singing with my chorus, but it's a disappointment. The design is 5 stars, but the fabric is stretchy nylon, making it hot and difficult to breathe through.

The design is terrific. It fits close to my face, without gaps, and the plastic strip inserted into the slot keeps the mask away from my mouth and provides plenty of room for my mouth to move, without sucking the mask in whenever I take a breath.

But that nylon fabric makes this mask impossible to wear for longer than a few minutes without needing to get fresh air.

This is made in China, and it ships directly from there, so it takes a long time to receive as well. And it had a funny smell and required washing before wearing, so you won't be able to hand these out at the beginning of rehearsal and expect people to comfortably wear them that night.

Others have posted positive reviews, so that makes me wonder if the manufacturer changed the fabric. If this was made from breathable cotton, it would be 10 stars!

rating 4.0. Don't buy a size larger all the time! Wish I bought a size smaller. I am 5'9" and 140 lbs. I usually wear M, so I ordered a L (because of all the comments about it fitting small. It is too big! I should have just ordered the M. The product looks just like expected, but it was too big for me. I am going to try altering it to fit. On a better note, the delivery was very fast (less than one week), and when I talked to the seller about the product being too big, they replied very quickly and courteously. If you are NOT ordering an XXL or XL, you might do better to buy the actual size you need, not a size larger.

This is a beautiful coat but I have to warn you-the quality was lacking although that's what I expected because it was so cheap. So when I got it I took it to a tailor and had the sleeves hemmed and the hood repaired (some stitches were missed), had to secure the buttons better and had it pressed (it was puckered and very wrinkled from the shipping) and for an extra 40 bucks I have myself a gorgeous new coat! rating 4.0. Pretty necklace, not the greatest quality. But good for the price! This is a really pretty necklace. The size is a lot bigger than I was expecting. I was thinking the peace sign would be about the size of a fifty cent piece, but it's about twice that size, more like the size of an Oreo. The chain is not great quality. It was a little kinked up when I got it. Also, some of the stones aren't set perfectly, so I'm worried they will eventually fall out. For the price, it is a great necklace. It's really cute and can go together with a lot of different outfits. It's great to spruce up a plain sweater or dress. I received this product in exchange for an honest review. I did pay the list price for it ($3.22 at the time of this review), so it wasn't really a promotion. All opinions and experiences are my own.

Since I just purchased this to wear to a 70's themed party with a particular coral/turquoise colored top, I may end up trying to paint the violet stones. For the price I paid, what can it hurt? I agree with the other reviews - the chain is ugly and cheap looking - I plan to trash it and replace it with a ribbon or something similar. Just remember - you get what you pay for.

rating 5.0. Better Than Expected! Love It. I almost didn't order this dress after reading the reviews but I'm glad I did.  I'm 5'8" and weigh 195lbs (yikes).  I just received it today so I haven't washed it yet and when I do I will only wash it in cold water and allow to air dry since it fits me now and I don't want to take the chance of it shrinking.  The chest is tight but not so much that I can't wear it.  With me being rather tall I expected it to be very short (judging by other reviews) but it comes to my ankles and will look great with sandals.  I'm also very pleased with the quality, no loose treads or frayed seams.  I purchased another dress from Amazon that looks a lot like this one from another company and it is terrible, I cannot keep the straps on my shoulders no matter how I try but the straps are perfect on this one.  As you can tell, I'm very pleased with this dress and would recommend to my friends.  I did NOT receive any sort of discount for this review

rating 5.0. I brought 4 dresses on a cruise but I just wore this one every day instead. This dress is soooo comfy and soooo cute. It is a thinner fabric but not sheer, it's just perfect for hot summer weather when you just can't bear the thought of putting on pants. The straps are adjustable which was great for me, I could shorten them all the way and make a nice midi-length beach dress, or let the straps out all the way and make an almost-floorlength dress for walking around the deck on my cruise. When I let the straps all the way out it was very low cut though so I did wear a sports bra underneath for more coverage. I could have easily dressed this up with a shawl or scarf and nice shoes, but it looked great with sandals or beachy flip-flips as well. Overall it is the purchase I am happiest about in preparation for my cruise and I might just purchase 4 more just like it for my next cruise. I just wish it came in a 100 colors so I never had to wear anything else!

rating 5.0. Durable, compact and lightweight. I've had this umbrella for about 5 months now, and I've had no issues with this umbrella. It opens easily and "closes" with one button. You still have to push the umbrella into the base to be able to deploy it again with the button, so just keep that in mind. This feature makes it really easy to get into your car with it without getting your other hand wet and possibly your head/outfit. I would definitely reccomend this umbrella if you plan on using it for only yourself, but it would not work well for a couple if you wanted to share. Also in really bad rainstorms where it's raining at an angle, you'll still get kind of wet because the umbrella circumference isn't that large. In that case, you'd want a bigger umbrella. This fits in a bag easily and is lightweight so it works great for me!

rating 2.0. Cheaply made piece of ---- I bought 3 of these at the same time, so I could always have an umbrella in my car, at work, and at home. The first one broke after 5 or 6 uses. rating 5.0. Great for Singing. Not So Great for Descending. It does a nice job of shielding the top of my dome from thousands upon thousands of irritating rain drops. I needed a replacement compact umbrella that would store nicely in my sport compact car. I can barely cram the kids in the backseat, so I need to make use of every cubic nanometer I can find. You may not ask, "Why did you need a replacement? Did your other one break? Was it defective?" Actually, neither! It held up in quite well during my unquantifiable length of use. "Unquantifiable?" you may ask. Well, yes. Due to its conveniently compact design, I managed to quite unintentionally, place it in a location undeterminable by sight, sound, smell, or bloodhound. I cannot attest to durability in a monsoon, but by firmly gripping the top of the shaft, I was able to angle the top in such away that it would not comically invert during a mild to moderate amount of wind. This will work nicely for an impromptu Gene Kelly impersonation, but probably not so much for a Mary Poppins re-enactment.

Pros:
Compact
Keeps the upper portion of you dry
Durable enough if you use strategery
Good enough for singing in the rain

Cons:
Compact enough to lose if you're inattentive
Not good for slow descent from high altitude

rating 3.0. Compact, but unstable This umbrella is very compact when closed, which is an enormous advantage for someone like me who usually stores everything in a backpack.

However, the umbrella struggles to remain open; even moderate winds will cause the umbrella to collapse on itself, which is incredibly frustrating. Furthermore, the height of the umbrella is too small, which means you have to raise it more than normal to fit under (and I am average height).

rating 5.0. Fabulous umbrella.  Light, very attractive, convenient and can be counted upon NOT to break down when needed!  Great purchase! Love this Totes umbrella!  After having purchased other non-Totes umbrellas and having them literally fall apart on me, i purchased this umbrella and it (we) have survived many a rainstorm!  It is nice and convenient because of its size.  If the day is sunny, i will usually leave the umbrella in my vehicle.  However, if there is even the slightest hint of any cloud formations for that day, this handy umbrella is my companion.  When i travel to Europe, i ALWAYS take this Totes umbrella with me.  Due to its size i can conveniently slip it into a side pocket of my backpack and it is ready for action as necessary.  Belgium of all places have pretty unpredictable weather, and you may have the sun out one moment and  a rain shower coming down when you least expected it.  It is light and attractive.  I really like the wide velcro tab which keeps the umbrella closed, it is stitched very nicely, versus the very small piece of velcro that is very commonly used in other cheaper umbrellas.  I am very happy with this purchase.

rating 4.0. Pretty, But not True Colored Blown Glass I have been working on minimizing my jewelry down to the few key items I really love and prefer to wear. I bought these to be my Blue Set (I got the earrings and the neckless). The earrings I received are a nice blue, similar to the ones shown on the sales page. They a little heavy in that I can feel them when I turn my head; but they do not pull down on the lobe so no fear of creating a huge elongated hole in my ear lobe.

The hook itself is sterling silver, so no swollen ear lobes for me!

The only thing that I did not realize when making the purchase was that the blown glass is clear. The coloring is added to the back. This does give a nice effect and the black backing. At first I thought the black backing was to help the coloring stand out. But as I look closer it, it seems to be to the back side of the coloring. I did scrape my fingernail along the edge of the black and I was able to scrape off a little coloring. However, even though I am disappointed that these are not colored glass I still find them quite pleasing. Knowing that the coloring is on the backside will keep me more mindful to take care of them.

Overall, they are pretty, they are sterling silver and they have some weight to them to keep them in place, but it is not colored blown glass.
