rating 4.0. I thought this was a nice bag. I took it to Europe and it ... I thought this was a nice bag.  I took it to Europe and it forced me to keep it light during the day.  I was able to fit a water bottle, phone, money, map, tissue and gum.  The only negative would be that the zippered parts could be larger.  They don't zip all the way across the bag making it a little difficult to get items in and out of those areas.

rating 5.0. Great LITTLE bag!! Love this little bag. It is very little but it's exactly what I wanted.  I take it to the gym as a mini gym bag to carry my workout gloves, locker lock and ankle straps.  Everything fits perfectly!  At first I didn't realize the bottom expands by unzipping - but when I figured that out, it makes it the exact right size.  It also has several handy pockets - 3 zipper pockets (one outside front, one outside back, one inside) and one open pocket right under the flap, and another two small organizational pockets inside.

rating 4.0. Just my style and size This little bag is great!  I use it for my everyday purse because I don't need anything that could carry a small child in.  This is perfect for my wallet, keys, and phone but also has a lot more compartments that I use occasionally.  The canvas and leather are sure to stand the test of time.  Unlike many other purses and bags on the market today, it shouldn't need replacing for a LONG time!  The only criticism I have is that the zippered compartment on the front flap has too small of an opening and goes down to deep to really be useful; I have small hands and it's a tight fit for me.  I'm worried if I put something there, I might not get it out again!

rating 5.0. Comfy, chic, canvas! I don't know why people are having issues with the color of the green- mine is a nicely faded olive drab, and it looks awesome! Nice thick strap, great hardware, great number and placement of pockets. The expansion zipper is a nice option to have! Wears very comfortably cross -body, wasn't shifting or twisting, even left it on through dinner out, almost forgot it was there!

Only issue I can foresee might be the Velcro closure - I'm super rough on Velcro, and it will stop 'grabbing' after some time. We'll see how this ages!
rating 5.0. Five Stars I absolutely love it....roomy spacious... ITs simple jst the right size!

rating 5.0. Perfect small messenger bag! Messenger back was small but perfect size for what I needed. Canvas is sturdy and a great color. I loved the pockets for keeping things organized. The adjustable strap is great for shorter or taller people (I'm 5'8" and it was perfect length). It seems higher quality that will be durable and last a long time for a great price. I'd recommend this to anyone who needs a small messenger!

rating 5.0. A traveler's companion! I brought this two nights before I had to leave for Italy, and it's the best impulse purchase I have made! Instead of carrying around a bulky backpack that could be pickpocketed, this small messenger bag is the perfect size that is not a hassle to carry but still has enough volume to carry the essentials like backup battery, map and tickets, and small water bottle.

Unless you just have to carry a lot of stuff that requires a backpack, this is a must for all travelers!

rating 4.0. This bag seemed like a great value First, I'm a man - I found myself in need of a small so-called 'Man Bag' capable of carrying my phone, wallet, keys, and an external battery pack. This bag seemed like a great value, especially with Prime Shipping (I tend to leave stuff to the last minute and needed it for a trip).
The construction is satisfactorily rugged, with a smooth liner enclosed by the canvas exterior. the green was much more subdued than the photo indicated, which was to its benefit for my uses.
My most significant comment is that a brightly-colored interior would've gone a long way to improving usability - stuff tends to shift and not be where I expect it, and unless there's a light source directly above the opening, it can be difficult to find something. a key strap might also be useful, so that they don't fall to the bottom.
the expandability was nice - when my wife unexpectedly tasked me with carrying a zoom lens (55-200 for the photogs reading this) for her DSLR, I was able to expand the bag and handle it without a problem.

rating 5.0. one of my favorite accessories ever I'd rate this more than 5 stars if possible.
I searched high and low for a *small* messenger bag that I could throw in a few essentials, but not be encumbered by a big tote. Essentials is the key word here - you're not going to fit in notebooks and wallets and tablets and phones, but I have no problem fitting my keys, cards/cash, phone, lip balm, an asthma inhaler, a pen and a small Moleskine notebook, and not have it look like it's stuffed to capacity.

I have carried this everywhere this summer - including music festivals - and it is beyond sturdy. I bought a bar of Otter Wax and plan on waxing the canvas so it a bit more resistant to rain (and soon, snow).

I could not be happier with my field bag!

rating 5.0. Sturdy, useful, and economical I bought this for an upcoming trip. While I haven't used it for that, I was blown away by the quality when it arrived. The canvas is incredibly sturdy, as is the twill strap. It's the exact size I needed -- will hold passport, phone, and a few sundries -- and I'm really looking forward to using it!  After the trip it will transition to my dog-walking bag, since it'll easily hold poop bags, flashlight, phone, and the dog's water bottle. The price really is great for such a well-made bag.

rating 5.0. Really good quality, handsome bag Really good quality, handsome bag. About the size you would use for daily or traveling. Doesn't hold a bunch of things, but just enough.
Good for men or women- color is really nice.

rating 5.0. Best Bag Ever for a Kindle or Kindle Fire!!! I got this bag for my new Kindle Fire (the new $49 model). [[ASIN:B018Y229OU Fire Tablet, 7" Display, Wi-Fi, 8 GB - Includes Special Offers, Magenta]] It's a perfect match. This bag is so darn attractive: slim and just big enough for a tablet or kindle. But it also has an outside zipper that allows the depth to be increase by an extra inch or so. Very unique idea!

Everyone who sees this bag loves it and asks me to buy one for them. Both colors (green & brown) and really nice - I have both - and I take turns with which one is my favorite, depending on the day . . .

rating 5.0. Worked great on vacation Worked great on vacation.  Fit a semi large wallet, checkbook and tablet as well as cell phone and tissues.  Had to work to get it all in, but the bag is sturdy and fit well, not in the way on our short trip.  Can't use for everyday, I carry too much "stuff".

rating 5.0. Great bag-perfect for travel This purse is the perfect size! I absolutely love it. I have a great leather handbag, however for running into the grocery store or grabbing lunch, it is overkill. I am able to keep this crossbody inside of my larger handbag along with things like Advil and other items I like to have with, but won't likely need on a quick trip. This bag is also perfect for traveling. It is secure when carrying crossbody and has room for my wallet, passport, cellphone and keys, plus more. Overall, this is a great bag and I would definitely recommend it.

rating 5.0. Great for Travel. Took this on a backpacking trip to Malaysia and it was perfect. rating 5.0. ... it difficult to put into words how much I love this purse I actually find it difficult to put into words how much I love this purse. I'm used to carrying larger bags, but have wanted to downsize for awhile. A trip to South America seemed like the perfect opportunity to minimalize a little bit.

I carry my wallet, cell phone, deodorant, a coin purse, and usually some gum and a phone charger without any difficulty. The purse also unzips to expand a little, providing some more space in the bottom.

My favorite feature is the crossstrap design. One strap goes over your head and you stick your arm through the other, allowing the purse to be worn comfortably and close to the body. This was particularly important to me, as I wanted to be secure when traveling (have heard of purse straps being cut on the subway and was not looking to become a victim).

I highly recommend this purse if you are traveling. I think the design is very secure.

rating 2.0. Looks good but not functional Not happy. I was hoping to use this bag when walking around town. The bag looks great but the strap bunches between the loops and this makes for an annoying experience. If you are putting a lot of small items in the bag (keys, wallet, phone, coins), you're not going to enjoy having to straighten the strap every few minutes when you reach for items in the bag. Ultimately couldnt use this bag on the trip and ended up being a total waste.

rating 4.0. Seems quality, but has that Made in China smell I like this bag. It is a smaller messenger bag; make sure you look over the dimensions to see if this will work for you. I like the size because it will fit my cell, my keys, a small wallet, and a few other small things... just the basics. There isn't room to over-stuff it, and turn it into a 20 lb. purse - this is a good thing imo.

I haven't had it long, but it seems very good quality, sturdy canvas. Doesn't feel cheap at all.

The one big problem I have with it, especially when I first opened the package, is the smell. It was made in China, and smells just like that - chemicals. I had to air it out for several days before I could even use it. I'd love to just throw it in the wash, but I feel like the faux leather details probably wouldn't hold up to that. I also found it interesting that I did not read a single review that mentioned the smell. Also, why does canvas smell?? I'm used to plastic products from China having the chemical smell, I didn't expect this bag to have it. It's been over a week, and while the smell is not as strong..it's still there. It's a shame because I wanted to start using it, as soon as I received it. It's a cute bag.

rating 5.0. A good no-frills deal. I paid ~$6 more to get the gold-tone (instead of the silver with blue highlights).

Either way, a no-frills great deal for the money. The alarm & the light are weak, but it's compact, nice-looking, & cheap.

The directions are self-contradictory -- one part of the manual says avoid getting it wet, but the words "water resistant" are right on the watch face. No problem wearing it in the shower.

BTW, I figured out how to keep the loop on the band in place -- a carefully-applied speck of super-glue in just the right (clean) spot works great. I'll leave it to you to follow up on this tip if you like.

rating 4.0. Great leggings. Different fabrics I have purchased these leggings in 2 colors. They are great! The pockets are great for carrying small things, especially if I am wearing these with a dress. I love the high waist too (actually high waist!). I'm not sure it is very tummy controlling, but that doesn't bother me.

The thing I don't like is that the fabric between the two colors was different! The purple was a delightful soft polyester blend that was warm and perfect for lounging in. The black was a standard nylon/spandex blend, which was disappointing because I wanted the other fabric. It is unclear from the description which leggings will be made out of which fabric.

That is the upside. this is the down side, they are disposable. Period. They snag VERY easily, they pill and they run like panty hose.  Yep, they run right up the front and the back and since most people do not wear underpants with them, this can be a problem.  I do not wear leggings like pants but, I do bend over without a care when I wear them.  Not these ones.  Be careful, be very careful.

rating 1.0. Tilt! Tilt! This is one of the most vulgar objects imaginable.  I wonder why I bought it!  It's very badly made, the design is impossibly awkward, the metal is so soft that the slightest pressure will break the loops, the "gems" don't fit the frames, the frames have little bumps intended to look like prongs, and aren't,  All I can think is that I thought the "gems" might be moonstonish from the picture, and they aren't - there are three different colors of gems, and not one resembles the picture.  My granddaughter is redesigning the necklace and there are enough gems to make another necklace, so for her amusement I'm keeping it.  And the ad copy is terribly misleading!  No purple, no shells, no bubbles, and certainly not a fragment of luxury anywhere.  Embarrassing.

rating 3.0. The color I received was not purply-pink like this one--but more deep royal blue-green with opalescent touches--which ... This necklace/earring set is dramatic!  The color I received was not purply-pink like this one--but more deep royal blue-green with opalescent touches--which was fine for me but if you're trying to match it to a particular outfit that might be a problem.  If you look closely at the necklace, it sort of looks like crumpled tinfoil under colored plastic, but this really can't be seen unless you are holding it in your hand and really staring at it.  I wore my necklace for a New Year's Even party with a plain black dress and recieved many compliments on my "outfit" although dress itself is quite boring and basic, this necklace seemed to turn it into a showstopper! One problem I had though was my semi-sensitive skin became irritated when I wore it once with perfume--I believe the combination resulted in sort of a chemical reaction.  It went away once I removed the necklace, so I'm pretty sure the necklace was the cause.  I left the perfume off my chest the next time and had no problems.  If you like dramatic, statement pieces, this ones worth the small investment!

rating 4.0. Beautiful set I love this necklace set!  The colors are brighter than depicted, change as the light hits them, and appear to be plastic pieces that are semi-opaque.  The metal is very very cheap and would undoubtedly turn my neck green if I wore it too long, but appears somewhat sturdy, and I don't anticipate that the chain or links will break easily or that the plastic bits will pop out of their settings.  The earrings are light enough that I don't notice them.  And what great trickery!!  My husband wanted to know if they were labradorite!  ( I actually have a similar blue-green labradorite necklace and it is about 2x heavier) I wore this set out for our anniversary, with a low cut black dress and they were gorgeous!!  Will be wearing them often, for dress up nights out.  They also arrived a whole week before anticipated, which was much appreciated.

rating 5.0. Amazed at the quality for this price! I am absolutely STUNNED by the quality of these sunglasses. More than decent, they're FABULOUS -- and ADORABLE!!! I will order more VivienFang for sure! I also love the presentation, which is fine enough for a nice gift. They are packaged with a lovely case, a screwdriver for your keychain and a nice lens cloth. Can't believe the value!

rating 4.0. Super cute I was very excited for these to arrive, and when they did I couldn't wait to try them on. First off, the box packaging these sunglasses came in are beautiful with a black tan and rose gold color scheme. Second, these glasses come with a very attractive black leather (or more likely faux leather) case, a cleaning cloth, and a little tool for fixing your glasses that comes on a convenient keychain. Now on to the actual glasses. They are super cute and for the most part everything I was looking for. I do have a few cons: these glasses are not as weighted as I'd hoped, meaning they feel a bit flimsy. If you were to skip using the case and just threw them in your purse, they might end up broken and/or scratched. Next, the lenses are not as reflective/mirrored/opaque and they appear in the photographs. In person, you can still see my eyes through the lenses which is a bit annoying. Overall I'm pretty happy with these sunglasses so far. The cons aren't a huge deal breaker for me especially if you take care to keep them protected in the case.

rating 2.0. Handle-capped in FL UPDATE: 7/27/17
Although I had quality issues with the set I ordered I would not hesitate to purchase a different model from this company due to the great customer service I received over the past two days. This company stands behind their products! I was offered a replacement set or full refund without hassle. I opted for the refund due to the bad experience I had the first time. I didn't have any confidence that a new set of the same model wouldn't have the same issues outlined in my initial review. I also want a luggage set with TSA approved locks. The refund was processed quickly and received before the estimated date. I appreciate the level of service I received and will consider other models/luggage sets from this company in the future.

Let's start with the positive! The luggage looks great, prime shipping, and value price point. I really like the red color and it makes it easier to spot at baggage claim. The luggage set came last week and this was my first time using it. Unfortunately, the handle broke on two out of the three bags before we even got to the airport this morning. I carried the largest peice by the side handle down my steps this morning and it just snapped off. The handle on the middle size piece snapped as I was lifting it to get off the shuttle on the way to the airport. Very disappointing way to start your vacation. I hope the other handles last until I get back home. I gave it two stars because of the color and the super smooth wheels. I hope the return process is not a hassle. I'm glad I saved the huge box they were shipped in.

rating 4.0. Saved my boots from being terribly uncomfortable! These pads seem to have saved my new booties from being horribly uncomfortable.. I tried the foot petals, moleskin...all did not work for this issue because the bottom of my heel was rubbing against the back of the boot, this is the only type of pad that could address this, because of the connection between the heel back and foot sole.

I like the smooth material of the pads and the adhesive doesn't ruin the inside of my shoe. However, it seems to have lost some stickiness after 1 adjustment so it's quite annoying to put my shoes on since the pad folds over. I may need to get another one to fix this. Once the shoe is on though, the pad is great. The gel cushion at the heel is a little firm , but I feel it gets softer with more wears.

I especially like the design of the pads because it protects the bottom of my foot - where the heel and foot bottom meet - which is an area many other heel pads don't/can't address.

rating 4.0. For Mens Dress Shoes! (Mens dress shoes) - Almost perfect.

I got new dress shoes, and within an hour, they were rubbing the back of my heels raw.

I bough pads from both Walmart, as well as a chain shoe store. Both were only small pads for the top/back part of the shoe. Neither stayed in place. These, with the extra bottom of heel area, make all the difference in keeping them in place.

For some reason, one pad didn't stick as well (on the heal area) as the other. This could very well have been an issue on my end, and is why I gave only 4 stars.

I used a Qtip to dab a little crazy glue in a couple spots to keep it in place. The other pad is working just fine.

rating 5.0. Life Changing! I love my Pavlok-- it's been the most effective alarm clock I ever used. I used to ignore alarms or stay lying in bed afterwards, scrolling through my phone for another half hour. Now with the risk of a zap, I'm up and on my feet right away to start the day. I'm still meaning to check out its other capabilities and coaching options, but it's already been totally worth the purchase!

rating 5.0. Finally!! a way to wake up without disrupting everyone else I have an unusual career where I need to be woken up multiple times in all hours of the evening and morning. The problem is, I have always had to set multiple alarms on my phone that wake me up AND my significant other!

I decided to try the shock clock 2 to see if it would be able to wake me up during my multiple alarms without also disrupting my significant other.

It works! I go to sleep with the Pavlok on and have multiple alarm times set through the app. I have them all set on vibrate (I wake up easily so I rarely use the zap) and it vibrates to wake me up at the 3 different times in the morning and my guy’s sleep isn’t disrupted at all :)

As an added bonus, this device also tracks my sleep so I can see how my sleep is each night as well!

I highly recommend it-- definitely one of the best purchases I have made this year for me and my relationship!

rating 5.0. Zap! I bought the product because I needed a "silent" alarm to get up without waking up my wife. The Shock Clock 2 can be set up to three mode: Beep, which is useless to me because I don't hear that high pitch sound anymore, vibration and a zap, which is a small electric spark. The intensity and combination of each mode can be adjusted to your liking. I have it set to vibration and zap. There is a 20s delay between vibration and zap and while I mostly wake up from the vibration there are days when I don't feel the vibration but certainly the Zap 20s later... Like the product. It's working well for me.
rating 1.0. G Cedía que era lo mejor pero sólo tiré mi dinero ala basura

rating 5.0. No issues for me I was concerned with buying this after seeing so many negative reviews. I've had zero issues pairing with my phone (iPhone). The beep is very very quiet. I thought there was no way it would ever wake me up and expected to have to get zapped in order to wake up. I'm a very deep sleeper and have purchased alarm clocks made for the hearing impaired before. They worked but also woke up anyone else in the house and probably the neighbors too. After using this for over 2 weeks now I'm amazed that most days I wake up to the barely audible beeping noise. Very rarely does it actually have to zap me to get me up. It can be adjusted to give more time between beep, vibrate and zap which I had to do as I couldn't find my phone and get it shut off before it'd zap me! Those who live with me are quite happy I purchased it as it doesn't phase them when I need to get up! I highly recommend this alarm for anyone that is a deep sleeper like I am who needs a good jolt to get them out of bed without waking the neighborhood.

The sleep tracking I don't think is very accurate as it goes solely on movement from what I understand so far. I think my fitbit was much more accurate at tracking sleep quality but I understand this is a new feature that they are still improving upon.

rating 5.0. If you're hard to wake... Try it out! The past 30+ years alarm clocks have not worked for me, no matter how much sleep, it was always hard to get started. This, for me, is not a watch, but a wrist worn alarm clock, and it works! I'm now getting up at 5:15am regularly for the gym and actually having a routine for once!

This watch doesn't show the time, and doesn't need to. It has vibrate, beep, and 'static' shock alarm, it can snooze or you can disable snooze and have to do jumping jacks to shut off the alarms.

The purpose of this watch (for me) is to Kickstart the body and mind with adrenaline immediately, which wakes you up and makes it hard to go back to sleep. You need some degree of discipline to use this appropriately.

- Keep your jeans high and tight!

I only used this for alarm clock purposes so never left the house, the copper metal painted chrome has worn off and now I have brown copper Mark's were I wore it the night before.
Its December 5 now and the replacement is pretty much broken again. Bluetooth stoped working so I cant connect to it to change times, so its stuck playing alarms at 4 am every day, wich is great through the week, not so much the weekend. I really love the Idea of this item but it so fragile. The app needed for this is great, but I cant use 90% of it cause it cant handle being anywhere but inside your house on your nightstand and even then it'll wear out in a couple months.

Stopped working altogether. Complete trash.

rating 5.0. Day 4 & the only downside is I should have started 6 months ago... This is only my 4th morning of my new routine with Pavlok’s Shock Clock 2...but let me tell you it is a GREAT 4th morning!! I have consistently struggled (and failed) with waking up alert & ready for the new day.  It isn’t that I cannot drag myself out of bed, just that it normally requires about 3 different alarm times & 30 or so minutes of “snoozing” those alarms before the realization kicks in that I am delaying the inevitable.  Day 1 I actually woke up 6 min before my scheduled alarm zap & forgot to turn the alarm off so I received the scheduled zap (have mine set for 5 consecutive zaps at 70%) & had to complete my 5 jumping jacks while at the back door letting my dogs outside for their morning “business.”  I am assuming I woke up ahead of schedule due to the lizard part of my brain not wanting to be shocked (literally).  Day 2 I barely felt the 1st of 5 zaps because I was in deep sleep BUT the 2nd one came through loud & clear & then I was up doing my jumping jacks.  Day 3 & 4 were basically the same as day 2 which is perfect.  I will say that the body’s natural response to the unexpected stimulus is what keeps you from getting right back in bed.  Of course the 5 jumping jacks help get the blood flowing too.  I do believe the adrenaline rush is what lingers & keeps you UP because I have never had a problem getting up for about 10 min in the morning to let a dog(s) out & then laying back down for that precious “10 more minutes” because my sleepy brain says those minutes will make the difference in a “good night’s sleep vs. a bad night’s sleep.”  With Pavlok’s Shock Clock 2 laying back down has not even entered my mind.  I am immediately awake/alert & ready to start checking boxes off my morning routine.  Even with it being dark outside so early in the morning the Shock Clock 2 has put a whole new spin on me OWNING MY MORNING!!

rating 1.0. JUNK This is literally crazy -- I just spent $150 on a really great idea that worked less than a dozen times. I work nights in the hospital and I needed something to replace my alarm. I used it less than a dozen times!! It connects to the phone but no shock/sound/etc. are elicited. I was attempting to return it at this time -- the return window closed 2 days ago! Please, please, please do not buy this product or anything from this seller.

rating 2.0. Over priced for a simple device The one reason why I got this was the zap feature to wake me up. Having to wake others to my alarms is a drag for them so I decided to get this. The device is cheap looking and the band definetly will not last long. The app is down right awful. For what they are charging for the watch should reflect in at least a decent app.

Watch
Pro:
-seems durable
-one full charge lasted me for a week
-will give you a good zap to wake you up
up
-I guess it can be used to fix habits but its misleading.
Cons:
-cheap band
-the device itself is cheap and clunky looking.
-holds a terrible connection with the app
-absolutely no manual. Seriously I get its 2019 but at least have a manual that comes with it. I can buy a $20 toy drone that has more features than this watch and it will come with a manual.

App:
Pro:
-has a multitude of feature for setting alarms. Zap, beep, and vibration. Multiple times can be set. Has options to zap you if you press snooze, have to perform jumping Jack's to stop alarm.

Cons:
-for me it's very cheap looking. At least stick with a pleasant color scheme.
-has features in it that don't make sense. I get you are trying to help with habits but that's what a trained and certified professional is for...focus on the UI and usability because it's very lack luster for a $150 device.
-if something is in beta just don't put it in...I paid a good amount of money for this and you are telling me these things are in beta...ouch
-video and sound is low quality and unnecessary for the tutorial.
-when I have the watch connected the app alone uses the second most amount of my battery...an unnecessary amount to put it bluntly.

To be honest I would not recommend this to anyone unless you absolutely need to wake up without a loud alarm. Leave the habit fixing to professionals do not depend on a finicky app and an overpriced electric shock toy to fix your habit. Right now there is nothing that's like it to compete with which is why I ended up buying this with the help of amazon points.

Overall great idea and concept but very poor execution.

rating 5.0. I now wake up immediately! I'd like to say, I'm an incredibly heavy sleeper. I would have to set an hour's worth (Sometimes more) of alarms to ensure I'd get up on time. My wife would inevitably end up waking before me, and I'd generally still only wake up to her kicking me. The first night I got this, I was skeptical. There are many bad reviews, and I was still worried I'd sleep through the shock. I was mostly correct to be worried to sleep through it, however, upon setting it to 100%, I do not sleep through it now! I've been waking up on the first alarm for about a week now. I feel like I'm getting much better sleep considering I'm not a groggy mess hitting snooze for an hour to two hours. It's incredible!
Also, to address the negative reviews, I'm mostly curious if people just don't understand how it works. I've had little to no issues. I've had trouble connecting twice, and all I did was turn off my phone Bluetooth and then turn it back on, and voila! It automatically connected upon my phone reestablishing Bluetooth. I haven't had a problem with it not registering that I have an alarm set. As a matter of fact, when I don't have it connected to my phone, it will still go off if I had an alarm set! Not to mention, I have only charged this once in a week, and it's still at 59% battery as of writing this. Granted, I only use it for sleeping. Needless to say, I'm beyond happy with my purchase and I'd recommend it to anyone!

Within minutes I received a response for steps to try but it did not work. The device was replaced with no questions asked, received the new one the next day and back on track to awesomeness! The quality of the device is really good as is the app. Being a newer company I expect some hurdles but they did great to correct it. It is true what the info says, as soon as I feel the vibration I am up as I know the zap is not far behind.

rating 5.0. Miracle socks. CHF patient approved. My step dad has Congestive Heart Failure (CHF) so he frequently experiences swollen feet, ankles, and when it’s really bad his legs too. We got a pair of these from a local sporting good store and gave them to him. His ankles and feet were already pretty swollen. He came back into the room and was pleasantly surprised that he had such an easy time putting them on over his swollen feet and legs. (Ive helped my mom put other brands on him when he’s really swollen. And it took a lot of strength from both of us. The fact that he could put them on by himself and they worked really well is an amazing thing.
I will recommend this brand of compression socks to anyone. I saw what it did for him and got myself and my boyfriend a pair too. We love them. I wear them for travel. I’ve slept in them before and did not wake up in pain like I have with other compression socks.

rating 1.0. Dangerous Product The upper band is too small and extremely restrictive.  There is no elasticity to the band of the socks that falls just below the knee.  After wearing the product for a day I was in severe pain after removing the socks. The socks when removed showed signs of choking off the flow of blood.  It was as if a tourniquet had been applied to my legs below the knee.  Until the product is changed individuals like myself will not be able to wear this product.  I am 6'4" and weigh 254lbs.  Copperfit compression socks need to come under review and the elastic band needs to be made larger for us big guys with large calf muscles.  An eight inch band is not going to stretch enough to accommodate 17inch plus calves.

I scoped the reviews multiple times, searching for reviews from petite girls in hopes that it would fit. I didn't have much luck. Despite that, took and chance and I'm pleased to say that it fit wonderfully with just a little wiggle room. You don't even notice. It's a beautiful dress and I recieved tons of compliments on it. The only complaint I have are the wrinkles on bottom half of the dress that I haven't been able to get out yet. Though they're not too noticeable. Besides that, I'd definitely recommend!  For reference, I'm 5'3, 33 bust, and 26 waist.

rating 4.0. They look great! I have been trying to find the impossible--good quality, color, and cut authentic diamond studs in 1/4 tw carat....that are under $400. It seems that few people want such small earrings but I would rather have that size; even if money was not an issue.  The best I've seen are graded as noticeably yellow. Therefore, I bought these though they are CZ's and larger than I wanted. That said, these seem to be adequate. I have always been able to tell real diamonds from cubic zirconias and while I can see these are CZ'S, I doubt anyone else could unless it was someone standing way too close! The posts seem fragile but I don't wear them everyday so I think they will be o.k. They were certainly well priced and they are pretty and reflect light beautifully. All in all, I am quite happy with these earrings while I continue my search for the small diamond studs that are out there somewhere, waiting for me. Until then, thanks again Amazon!

rating 5.0. Would purchase these again! These are a great workout tank to have. I have worn them many times and am very satisfied. The white one is more see through than I care for, but the quality is still nice. They keep me feeling cool, but aren't necessarily moisture-wicking. They have held up nice in both the washer and dryer--no fraying or coming apart at the seems. They also do not ride up on me when wearing them at home or during a workout, which is a huge plus. I have had issues with shirts like that in the past, and it is very uncomfortable. They seem to be a tad longer than some shirts, so that is probable why they perform so well. Sadly these were bought right as I found out I was pregnant, so I can't wear them for very long, but I'll enjoy it while it lasts!

rating 5.0. Perfect for trigger finger Excellent. These things are better than gloves, for me at least. They restrict finger movement just enough to keep it from locking in that claw-like position, sparing me the unlocking and annoying, sometimes painful, upward fling. No finger pain to wake me up. I can put one on the single finger that hurts. I don't know if this will restore the finger to normal operation, but I've had various fingers and a thumb lock up over the years. Eventually they go back to normal, but it's annoying until they do, so these are great to have.

For years I've tried various gloves, supports, whatevers, but these are the best.

rating 4.0. Little hugs for my fingers :) Comfy for my sore finger joints, not super tight, more like a tiny hug. For the price, though, I am really satisfied.

rating 5.0. Inexpensive and they work For almost a month I was in excruciating pain in my middle index finger which would not go down. In Califonia that qualifies as "driving disabilty." I used various herbs, pain killers but found that if I put this on my finger (as I did with another brand) it felt good during the day and seemed to slowly heal. I'm now about 95% l healed this finger. There is no magic wand on this kind of problem. It's incremental healing. These do a great job.

This dress is great. It's a heavy fabric material and very stretchy but without that "show all your flaws" you get from a thinner material. The waist set slightly below the boobs and the length is below the knee. At first I felt like the dress might be too long by an inch or so but I decided I was wrong. I'm pretty sure it's exactly the length it's supposed to be I'm just not used to below the knee. With my bust it does do the normal button up problem with boobs but it has two layers for hiding the buttons so using a safety pin will be easy as will just adding an extra button.

current_product_reviews:
rating 2.0. Looks/fits great, bad quality/workmanship Let me start by saying I am five foot one, with the hips of a woman who has six kids. So I was looking for something that would keep all that to a minimum lol
It was badly wrinkled in the package and I had to steam it before ironing it. No big deal. Material is thick, sturdy with some stretch. Sleeves were a tad too long, but again, no big deal. The skirt hits me an inch or so below my knee, and is very full without a crinoline beneath it...very vintage looking. The issue came when I washed it (on gentle) to rid it of that slight chemical smell that new clothes have. Two inches of the seam right below my waist line came unstitched. The collar wrinkled so bad I cannot iron it out. The lace is unraveling and has strings hanging all over the place. I fixed everything but the collar, and wore it a two more times. I was afraid to wash it, but my toddler wiped her face on the skirt at a funeral and I had to. More seams came undone. There are three separate seams that need fixing now. The lace is not holding up well. I am not pleased with the workmanship on this dress, and find that it is over priced for the quality.
However, it is a beautiful dress. I was complemented numerous times all three times I wore it and everyone wanted to know where I had found such a gorgeous dress. It was comfortable to wear, no itchy or scratchy issues with the lace. I could be tempted to buy this again, if the price were closer to $20 than $45, as I figure I'd probably have to resew most of the seams. Otherwise, I'd just try another brand. I just don't see the point in spending a ton of money on anything that constantly needs to be fixed because of poor workmanship.
UPDATE:
I fixed the seams and wore it to church, where my husband pointed out a huge hole in the armpit. Once home, I examined the hole and discovered that whoever sewed that particular part of the dress missed the fabric. There is a two inch bunch of stitches that hangs off the dress by the hole. I'm taking a star away for deplorable workmanship, and will NOT be buying this brand again, regardless of price.

For my 9 year old, these are too big.  They slip down on her nose a little, but it will work for the play she's in.  Also, I think the picture on the website used to be different. Again, the glasses are okay for a costume, but I thought the ad was a little misleading in terms of frame color and suggested age range.

I could have potentially got a medium if I wanted the fit to be a little tighter like a sports bra. No issues with riding up or anything with a size large. Only issue is that the back closure is like a bathing suit. There's a loop that slides onto a hook (not like a normal bra) and I'm afraid it'll slip off when wearing it. I plan on just sewing it closed and pulling it over my head. Really like that foam cups in this bralette which I didn't expect so nipple coverage and support isn't an issue for me.

rating 4.0. Overall, a good quality for the price. Compares well to my Chaps go to pant. I am a 33 year old female, and looking professional is what I have to do Monday through Friday as a DON of an organization. My husband would also say that I am very particular in my choice of clothing and since neither being a teen or baby boomer I have a hard time fitting into the clothes that I want without being too revealing. I usually am a size 10, size 39/40/41 inch hip depending on how much candy I have indulged on. Since my go to professional pants (Chaps stretch straight leg sz 10 $59) is no longer being sold locally I decided to shop on here. I was a sceptic about these pants at first, but on a whim after reading reviews I sized up to a L, with a 32 inch inseam. I also have long legs and some of my pants look short on me after a few washes.When I received these they are well stitched and also a bit of a thicker and more professional material. I pulled them on and they are loose enough to look flattering but tight enough to stay sharp. They sit on my natural waist, and hit below my ankles with enough fabric to allow for shrinking in height, if that happens. The only downside is the back pockets are not real and a belt loop came loose as I pulled them on. Overall, a good deal. As a side note I feel I don't have the smoothest or nicest backside in the world but these pants flow over it well.

rating 2.0. Low quality, seam ripped after one weweu I do love the fit and style of this pant, and the material would be cool for summer, however, the quality is lacking. after wearing them once and washing them, I got them out today to wear them for only the second time to find there is a 2 inch hole along the inner seam where the seam has separated or one side has pulled away because it wasn’t sewn right. Because I’m outside my 30 day window, it seems I can’t return or exchange.  That’s a real disappointment to only get to wear a pant once before it fell apart. I have this pant in a larger size and I’ve worn them 4-5 time without issue, so maybe it’s not an issue with all pants.

rating 2.0. A few design flaws Ended up returning these. They would be pretty comfortable and I'd probably be able to get away with wearing them to work, but there were two issues I really didn't like. First, having functional front pockets is great, but with the odd stiff/stretchy fabric I found that the inside lining of the pockets showed through too much. Second, the pants are cut so that the waist is smaller than the hips. This is actually great for avoiding the dreaded waist gap, but the problem is that the pair I received had non-stretchy thread in the waist. I honestly can't believe I didn't see another review mentioning this, so maybe it was just my pair. Regardless, I was afraid I was going to rip the waist seam when I tried to get them over my hips and they weren't stretching to fit over the booty, so back they go.

rating 1.0. nice studs, ball of puss after couple weeks of use. When the nose studs came in, I was so happy! I loved how they came in properly packaged, properly placed in a case. I was happy for the size, just what I wanted, was perfect! Although, the ball of the nose stud is a bit too big. I've been using one of the nose studs for couple weeks. I would often clean around the nose stud with alcohol wipes, just to keep it clean and took out the stud once which was last week just to make sure my nose pierce was okay and the stud wasn't rusty (because I saw feedbacks on here the the nose stud got rusty after being on for couple weeks and another comment where her nose got infected after using the nose stud) well this morning I looked in the mirror and saw a ball of puss above the nose stud, i immediately took off the stud and there sit a huge ball of puss on my piercing of my nose, I tried to wipe it off, thats when I realized I have to pop it, I got kleenex and popped the ball of puss, alot came out and blood started coming out. My nose piercing is still leaking of blood and some puss. I was going to post a picture but I don't feel comfortable showing it. I am embarrassed to go out in public with a BIG hopefully not infected piercing on my nose, thankfully the mask will keep it covered. I never had a problem with my nose piercing, with my previous nose studs, they were fine. Nothing crazy like this happened.. until now. Maybe its the type of studs? Maybe the big ball? I am not sure but I am not happy anymore. They are very nice! But not after a period of time being used, well not for me anyway.

current_product_reviews: rating 1.0. Major discomfort and discoloration I’ve had my nose pierced for 11 years so I’ve had MANY nose rings. I generally purchase a pack like these and they last for a year. When these come in I put one in any holy shit it hurt. Similar to the first time you switch out your original piercing for a replacement piece. I’ve had it in for a few weeks and noticed today Major discoloring of the nose ring and my nose piercing itself - both black. I started to remove it to replace with a new one and it hurt like hell AGAIN. I got out a new ring and looked at it closely and it’s the ball on the end that’s the issue. It’s way too big. It’s bigger than the small stone on the other end. Trash. Throwing them all out and ordering new ones. Do NOT recommend!

rating 4.0. Nice Inexpensive Versatile Dress For Busty Curvy Thick Ladies This is a cute, breezy, little dress. It doesn't cling to your body. It hangs beautifully and flowy. The arm holes are perfect. They don't hang and aren't too tight. Bra straps do not show at all.

I am 5'6", 170lbs, 38 D. I also have a long torso and long legs. I purchased size XL just in case it was sized smaller.! The dress hits just above my knees. Not so long that it's an awkward length. It's just perfect for breezy days outside or even for a trip the beach, which is what I purchased it for - beach trip. I purchased the color that appears to be an Army Green color or some sort of green. However, the dress is BROWN NOT GREEN AT ALL. Not even kinda sorta green if you wink your left eye and tilt your head to the side. It's still BROWN. DEFINITELY BROWN. DARK BROWN. I wouldn't have purchased this color had I known it was nothing close to what it appeared to be as far as color goes. I'm not into a little brown dress. BUT, I've decided to keep it because of the way it looks on a thick body, the way it feels on the skin, as well as some great ideas I've come up with to wear it that I'll share below.

I do feel very comfortable in this dress. I could've probably gone down a size, but I'm happy with the way it fits a little looser so I'm not self conscious about my little extra tummy. Yet it's not so loose that it makes me look like I'm wearing a big sack or moo-moo. You still have your curves and don't look huge! This is definitely a great little dress for ladies with curves!

I wash the dress turned inside out by itself or with a few pieces that are similar material or colors. I was on delicate with cold water only. I DO NOT WASH THIS WITH ANYTHING THAT MAY SHED to avoid pilling on the fabric. I usually toss it in the dryer on AIR DRY (no heat) for about 10-15 minutes. Then i lay it across a bar stool or my couch to let it air dry. I don't want to hang it and have the straps stretch too much. It dries VERY quickly. I either fold it or hang it depending on how soon I plan on wearing it again. I will wrinkle a bit when folded but the wrinkles fall out EASILY if thrown into the dryer for a few minutes on AIR DRY. There is no reason to iron it. And honestly it can be worn without air drying it at all because the wrinkles fall out of this fabric so often.

All in all I like the dress. I'm just not wild about the color. I can work around the brown by wearing it with a long necklace that hangs pretty low along with some other boho style jewelry (leather strap bracelets that wrap around your wrists or leather bracelets that snap) along with sandals. I've also thought ahead to fall and thought this would be so cute with one of those large "blanket" type scarves. I have one that's a cream color with some large plaid. I think this would look darling using a similar scarf or any coordinating large scarf with tall boots and some tights. Another fall look could be a chunky cardigan along with tights and tall boots that says "I just threw this on and still look cute". It could also be paired with a short denim jacket for an easy, trendy look. This drew would also look great with some funky, fall, Aztec patterned leggings. I'm actually going to start looking for some now!

Those are just some ideas for small accessories and basic shoes most everyone has or can find easily, and pretty cheap, that could change this dress to make this a darling, versatile piece that can be worn many different ways.

Overall, for this price and the various ways to turn this simple dress into so many different looks its ABSOLUTELY worth every penny. I may purchase this in another color. I definitely recommend this to busty ladies (its not tight across the chest at all and not baggy). It's also great for curvy or thick ladies.?

I do advise using the size chart in order to get the correct size. Nobody asked me to write this review. Nobody gave me this dress to write a review. Nobody gave me a discount to write this review. I purchased the dress myself and this is truly my honest opinion about it.

input:
rating 5.0. Very handsome casual watch I had never heard of Skmei or CakCity before this week. I looked for customer reviews for the brand on websites other than Amazon, and found some things that seemed a little sketchy. There are at least 3 web pages, under different domain names, that come up in a Google search for Skmei watches customer reviews, but don't have any actual reviews. They're nothing but advertising. At the price I paid for this watch, however, I'm pleased so far.

I've had the watch for about 3 days, and it's keeping good time. I really like the way it looks. It's nice-casual, not for dress-up occasions but very handsome for casual wear. I especially like the band. I've had troubled relationships with watchbands in the past, but this one looks sturdy and well-made enough to last. It fits my wrist, which is about 7.5 - 7.75 inches around. It wouldn't be long enough for a wrist more than about a quarter to a third of an inch larger than that.

rating 1.0. Keep away from ANY amount of water I bought this watch with high hopes.  I was washing my hands in the bathroom and the smallest amount of water sprayed over the supposedly water resistant watch and it went in! Like literally went in the watch and fogged up the glass.  Embarrassing and a waste of $16.

rating 1.0. Not even right twice a day Straight out of the box the time is wrong. When the hour hand points at 12, the minute hand points at 6! It boggles the mind. Is there a way to fix that? It appears that the hour hand is off by 30 minutes relative to the minute hand. Otherwise appears work.

rating 5.0. It's such a quality piece and an amazing bargin! I paired this $15 CakCity watch in gold with a $10.50 iStrap gold deployment clasp and a $34.95 Clockwork Synergy red vintage leather watch strap from their Dapper collection. Each of these available at Amazon currently at the prices I've stated.  It's now one of my favorite watches among my twenty plus  collection of much greater value.  It looks great and keeps perfect time.  It's  such a good quality piece and an amazing bargain.  The watch strap is silky soft and supple, high quality leather and shows that, which adds to the image and feel of this piece.
rating 1.0. Disappointed! Though the watch looks good Disappointed! Though the watch looks good, I received it without a proper box, description or warranty! It cannot be an appropriate gift!

rating 5.0. comfortable and the case thickness at just 0 Although I own several watches, I find myself wearing this watch on a daily basis, whether I dress formally or wear shorts, this inexpensive watch has won me over. Very stylish, comfortable and the case thickness at just 0.6 CM is one of the best watches I've owned at a fraction of the cost I've paid for other bulkier, less stylish watches I've purchased. If you're sitting on the fence on this watch, go for it, you won't regret it both in price and style.

rating 5.0. I am pleased to say that the watch met every expectation that ... I ordered the watch about about a month ago, wasnt sure what to expect with it being a mere $15, but my hopes werent exactly high. I am pleased to say that the watch met every expectation that I had, and more. Its a very good looking watch, looks much more expensive than it actually is, and I've gotten numerous compliments on it. I currently use it as my daily watch, and I am highly satisfied. I would recommend it if youre looking for a cheap, good-looking alternative to some of the more expensive watches on the market.

input:
rating 5.0. Gee, I Hope These Come Back I'll repost when I find out how they wear, but it's like putting your foot in warm butter. They're the merino dress sock I've been looking for so far.
Tried to order these again for next year. They're perfect for a warm lightweight sock and I live in the western Upper Peninsula of Michigan!

rating 5.0. Well worth purchasing these merino wool socks and a great value. I just love these black merino wool socks.  They fit as expected and keep my feet warm.  I don't like a very thick sock - just the right thickness.  I'm as happy I ordered these sock.  When I need to order more socks, I will order these socks again.  Well worth the money.
rating 4.0. Nice and thin wool socks Nice and thin wool socks.  Keeps my feet warmer than my usual cotton socks in the office.  Use something thicker for outdoors.

rating 2.0. Black socks shuffle This is the fourth time I have bought these socks. The first 18 pairs were soft, warm, and a pleasure to wear. This iteration is totally different. First of all the size is inconsistent with the prior orders. They are too big for a woman’s size 6-9.
The fabric is totally different and feels quit synthetic. I was quite disappointed and will be returning them and seeking an alternative before winter. Not to say they are bad socks, they are not what I ordered and expected.

rating 5.0. I’m In love! I truly am a sock connoisseur! I never wear anything, clothing-wise, that is not comfortable and just taking these socks out of the package made me so happy that I bought them. They are soft and stretchy, not too thick or too thin. They feel fantastic on my feet!

rating 5.0. Wonderful socks! I bought these based on reviews and I am not sorry--as sometimes happens. The socks are very good quality, wash well, keep their shape and are just the right weight (medium I’d say) to be warm without being boot socks. I line dry them as I do all my wool stuff, even if it’s maching washable.
rating 1.0. Developes holes in the heels I loved the socks, but they develop holes in the heels way too soon.

rating 5.0. Great wool socks Great light to medium weight wool socks for women.  They really add warmth when wearing with my normal shoes.  I haven't had a chance to wash them yet, so I will update my resume and if any issues.

I burned a piece of loose yarn and compared it with some from a Smartwool sock with the same material content to compare. Smartwool left soot and this had none, just melted.  Wishful thinking at this price.

rating 5.0. The best socks I have worn in years!1 I was sick of buying cheap socks and finally decided on these socks that were a bit more than I would usually pay. after all, they go in your shoes. well, I must say I might be weird but I looooooveee these socks and I love putting them on and taking them off. so soft and so comfortable and warm! I can't wait to see how my feet feel in winter when wearing these. I am thinking of purchasing another set. it is amazing! I love them. I would recommend. I haven't washed them yet but I hope they hold up. These are my new faves. I wore them in boots and did not feel the usual uncomfortable front end rubbing against my foot in those boots. No pilling,

rating 5.0. Treat your feet to tapping toes - this is a great sock! Excellent purchase - great value, well-made socks!  So tabs or bumps in fabric hitting you in the toe and making shoes uncomfortable - just the opposite, GREAT SOCKS!!!  Thin, warm, stay up - what more could you want for your feet?

rating 5.0. Great weight wool socks, wash and wear These are fantastic socks. They are the perfect weight to wear with normal shoes, sneakers and such, and warm and dry because they're wool. I have machine washed and dried (cold water/low heat) them 3 or 4 times now and they're still lovely and soft. I'm going to buy my sister a pack for Christmas!

rating 4.0. Soft to the touch Socks are soft and thin, just as I wanted.  They are a blend of  mostly cashmere, and feel lovely.  Wanted something warm yet without bulk, and so far, in a mild fall, they are fine.  The only reason they are rated 4 stars is that they sagged down my calves as I wore them. Don't know if that's because I used them with a brace or not.  Otherwise I like them, and would recommend them.

rating 5.0. Best socks I ever owned Wish I had discovered these years ago. Best socks I have ever owned. Super soft. Great trouser socks for work. Feet stay warm and dry (unlike cotton or synthetics).

rating 5.0. Toasty These socks are soooooo soft and comfy. They keep my feet warm in the gross winter weather without getting itchy or sweaty. I've also noticed less foot odor, even after a long day with my feet stuffed into boots!

rating 3.0. Comfortable and soft, but, shed a lot! Very soft and comfortable, but they do shed/pill! So, they look kind of ratty after wearing them only once. I haven't washed them since wearing them, so I'm not sure how they will hold up after washing. The pic on the far left is new from the package and the other two are after wearing once for about 8 hours.

rating 5.0. Keep my feet warm and cozy, yet breathable I’ve been using these socks for a couple weeks now and I really enjoy them. My feet are so much more warm and cozy (but thin enough so my feet also breathe) now that I’ve switched to the wool blend. Delivered fast and easy. Highly recommend.

rating 4.0. Update... 9 months later I really love these. They are perfect for many types of shoes. I wish they had then in knee highs!

After a few months of wear, I noticed that the big toe got little tiny holes in them. Now, just about every sock has worn through. They are great socks when you first buy them, but they do not last. I wear them everyday to work during the school year, so I didn't even wear them during the summer months. I am disappointed and will try to find another comfortable brand that will hold up.

rating 3.0. Update: 16 months later I did love these socks, but they’ve started to wear out faster than they should have. I’ve tossed at least 2 or 3 pairs with holes in the heel. I don’t regret the purchase, but just realized these aren’t for the long haul.

Original review:
You'll love these so much you'll want to wear them every day. They're warm and comfy but also thin enough that I can wear them with my Birk clogs or winter boots. They wash up great in my normal washer and dryer loads- no careful laundry instructions. I'm anxiously awaiting more colors and styles so I can justify buying more.

rating 2.0. Disappointed I was very pleased to have thin wool socks for the cooler Florida days.  However, the first pair that I put on quickly developed a hole in the heel - on the first day worn - and had to be tossed out.  I hope the other pairs do better.  I would not re-order.

rating 5.0. Excellent Merino Wool Combo Socks! Being a daily hiker, rain or shine, I developed a couple is issues with my feet. I was looking for a really soft, breathable, moisture wicking sock as they were healing. I was skeptical about this product but because Amazon has such a great return policy I took a chance. Other than the socks are a little thicker than I wanted they are perfect in every way! And with winter approaching, they will be excellent for winter hiking and cross country skiing. rating 5.0. Perfect Snowboarding/Skiing Socks After a recommendation from someone at my local ski shop to wear very thin wool socks to keep my feet warmer, I came across this affordable 6 pack. Can I just say, HOLY COW! I've always had problems with cold feet and my toes going numb while snowboarding. Not anymore though! I never understood why I experienced this since I would buy good quality & thick wool socks that were perfectly warm when I wasn't wearing a tight boot.

THE GOOD STUFF:
- the thin sock allows room for air to flow between the foot/sock and the boot.
- this trapped air keeps your feet and toes nice and toasty ALL DAY LONG!
- I no longer have trouble squeezing my foot into my boot, because the thin sock leaves a little bit more room.
- I now use these around the house too, not just for snowboarding. They're great everyday socks for the winter!
- the 6 pair pack is perfect for a ski trip, without having to worry about washing your socks while you're away.

THE BAD STUFF:
- When worn alone around the house, with no shoes or slippers, my feet feel a little cold. This is when I'd opt to wear a nice thick pair of socks instead. But, just put on a pair of slippers and your feet will be back to a nice and toasty temperature!
- I wish there were more color options to choose from. I'm a fan of fun, patterned socks!

rating 5.0. Soft, warm, very washable These are great! I wanted socks I could wear year round, all the same color and that would keep my feet warm and cozy and wick away moisture. These are great! I live in the mid-Atlantic and during winter I double the socks in my rain boots and I’m toasty. I wear them all day indoors and to bed. They’re wonderful! I even wore them on occasion in the summer for exercise and my feet stayed dry and comfortable. I don’t wear socks in warm weather most of the time so I only have these socks in my closet and they’re perfect!

rating 4.0. ~7.25" = max wrist size I've had it on for less than an hour, so I'll edit my review after it's gotten some real usage, but for those concerned about the band size:
The user guide says not to wear it too tight; you should be able to slide one finger underneath the band while it's on your wrist.

It fits my ~6 inch wrist just fine.

For the benefit of others looking into this watch, I loosened it and fastened the buckle in the last hole and slid it up my forearm until it fit, but while I was still able to fit a finger underneath the band and my arm.

You should probably never buy a watch that only fits using the last hole. With this watch, the loop that holds the end of the band in place can't even cover the band end when loosened to this degree.

The largest wrist this could *accommodate* would be 7.5" in diameter.

The largest wrist this could fit comfortably on would be below 7.25"

input: rating 5.0. Satisfying :) I really enjoy this product. It's extremely lightweight, and fits snugly on your ear -- you don't even feel it on your ear at all. I sleep and shower with it, it's comfortable, doesn't get caught in my hair, and /hugs/ the ears! Not the mention, the packaging is just adorable. I highly recommend it to anyone who's looking for a small pair of hugging loops that is both comfortable and stylish for casual and formal occasions!
rating 5.0. The best yet These are the best buy of three pairs I puchased.  These are well made and I would order other huggy earrings made by this manufacturer.

rating 4.0. Pretty hoops, but small I do like these earrings. The diamond cutting catches the light nicely. It's just that I didn't expect them to be so tiny. I expected them to be petite, but not tiny.

rating 5.0. Sterling Silver Polish Hugies I'm very picky with my earrings. If I wear hoops, I like them to hug right up to my ear. These are absolutely perfect. They fit my bottom hole perfect and hugs my ear like I wanted it to with very little space left over. These are the perfect size!

rating 5.0. good for big people too marketed for children, for which they would be very cute, but i have a 3rd hole that needed an understated earring....this fit the bill perfectly.  it fits snugly on my 'big girl' ear, with no space between the lobe and the earring....exactly what i was looking for.  earring is beautiful, to boot.  high quality and highly recommended, if you are looking for a very snug little earring.

rating 5.0. Very cute, but very small I purchased these for my self. As an adult female I have a small to average face/head so I thought these would be perfect for me. They are great for someone who likes wearing earrings even while working out or doing insignificant things.I like wearing these better than wearing studs because they will not fall out easily.

rating 5.0. Delicate and pretty. I read one review, which pointed out these are tiny not petite. That is correct. They look just like they are shown in the picture advertising them. rating 5.0. Huggie Earrings At work we were discussing huggie earings, I had never heard of them. I wasn't sure what size to get and what hole in my ear I would wear them.

I would recommend this to anyone who wants a small earring to where in the top hole if they have more than one holes or in there primary hole.
Enjoy

rating 5.0. All function with just enough form to have become an enduring, timeless classic timepiece For the right kind of person, with just exactly the right sort of style (or appropriate lack thereof), the Casio F91W is every bit as good as, if not better than, a Cartier. Th F91W is a classic. It's remained virtually unchanged for decades, and there's a reason for that. Certainly, it has its own look. It's slim, sleek, and sports the cleanest of clean lines. It's simple, and it doesn't make a big fuss. It's comfortable, it's accurate, and it does enough. Its alarm is positively reliable, and its stopwatch is simple and consistent. It's small and unassuming, and doesn't draw a lot of attention to itself because it doesn't need to. This watch exists for its wearer and no one else. It doesn't try to impress, but it will never let you down. It does a few things and it does them well, all the while, not pretending to care at all about the things it doesn't do.

It's a perfect watch for a woman or a man, for a child or an adult. It's good for sport, and it's good for relaxing, and it's good for work, and it's just fine for everything else in between.

If this watch were a person, when asked the time, it would reply "quarter to three!" with a big grin on its face, and go back to its drink without missing a beat. It wouldn't huff and fumble for the time and act as if you were intruding on it before somehow giving you a time that was an hour too fast or slow.

But you knew that already, didn't you? It used to be that you could find an F91W accidentally, just out shopping in the department stores. And maybe you'd need a new watch so you'd pick one up. But today, and especially online, the F91W doesn't really find you as much as you go looking for it. You wouldn't be looking at it if you didn't already know it was perfect for you. And if it's not the watch for you, that's fine too, just move along to something else. The F91W isn't bothered. It knows who its people are.

rating 1.0. Faulty Casio watch not returnable; only 1 month warranty. rating 1.0. too few features, some flaws I am very disappointed in this watch, the F-91W.

I have owned a different watch, the Casio W-71 for many years, and find it to be a versatile watch, but the band just broke (as they do after several years). I purchased the F-91W awhile back as a future replacement, but found it lacking seriously when I had to use it. I would have sent it back, but considering the cost of shipping, it was not worth it.

The features are scaled back compared to the W-71. There is no count-down timer, which I use almost daily. The button that cycles through the features goes through the "change time," making it easy to change the time by mistake. (The button layout is not convenient for someone who uses the different features multiple times daily, but I concede that may be my bias.) I also use the feature to set an alarm for a specific date (the one day I have to catch a plane at 6 am), so I do not forget and get awakened at 4 am for the next few days. There are more limitations and flaws in addition to these I have listed.

Pros:
cheap
its a Casio

Cons:
too easy to change the time by mistake
limted features
no month on date, just the day (fine, my pet peeve)
no count down timer
single alarm mode
(button layout not as easy to use)

The W-71 is about twice the cost, but well worth it. I am going to find a replacement band (or two) for my old one, and put this F-91W back in a drawer.

Spend the extra $10 for a much better watch. Stock up on replacement bands at Wal-Mart or such and it will last for many years.

rating 5.0. This is a Worthy and Charming Classic from The 70's-'80s This smallish, but capable watch is a perennial favorite for those, who looking for a reliable and capable watch at an outstandingly reasonable price. Sure there are cheaper watches available on the market, but for a few dollars more than those, the buyer of this timepiece shall get a well established, reputable name, with the rock solid quality to match. The aesthetics of the watch is an added bonus for those, who like its low key, yet charming appearance. To me the only area open to criticism is the two piece stock band. Although it blends-in well with the overall design scheme, its quality doesn't quite matches up. Still, considering the price-range this watch is selling for, my criticism has to be muted. For example, (after a minor modification), I was able to replace the resin band with a more satisfactory  one piece NATO/Zulu style strap, with its sturdy metal buckle and keepers, however this cost me just about twice as much, what the watch together with its stock band cost me originally.

rating 1.0. I have no complaints about thewatch itself, it does its job I have no complaints about the watch itself, it does its job.  But the watchband is trash and deserves the waste basket.  The one I got was too short for my wrist, and I am not a large male.  The buckle is plastic with a plastic pin instead of metal.  This means that if the tiny short end of the plastic pin falls off, you have likely lost your watch.  The end of the pin is only about one eighth of an inch in length and one sixteenth of an inch thick.  I have seen these tips break off and that is all that is holding the watch to your wrist.  It is planned obsolescence.  The resin band on a fairly new one I purchased half broke within about a year.  That is another quick way to lose your watch.  Maybe a thicker band would help.  But I think this watch company is doing a great disservice to its customers with such a trashy watchband, and I can only think they know what they are doing and doing it on purpose.  I can only give Cassio the lowest rating.

rating 5.0. Great watch!!! (Just try to ignore its infamous association) Elegant, accurate, thin watch, and excellent bang for the buck. The optional hourly chime reminds me of my childhood. As another reviewer noted, anyone who complains about this watch doesn't know how good a watch can be for $10. This is one of the few digital watches that is thin enough to not snag on my long sleeve work shirts (and I button them with the looser button). I have this great system: I wear the A158W-1 (same watch but in shiny stainless steel; looks like it's worth $100!) for work or more professional/formal occasions, and this one for casual or sports. For not much more than $20 for the two watches, it's an awesome system for me and I love the modesty, basic functionality, and what I call an "anti-hoity toity" feeling!

Press and hold the right lower button for 5 seconds until it says "Casio" in digital letters to verify it's authentic.

After I purchased it I was reading about it on a Casio collector's website and was disappointed to discover that this watch was worn by the most infamous terrorist of our time (whom I will not name; he can be seen wearing this watch in photos) and has been favored by them. I try to ignore this awful association and just enjoy the watch. Some websites have even suggested that if you wear this watch, you might be suspected as being one, which I find far fetched: http://gizmodo.com/5795554/people-wearing-this-casio-watch-might-be-terrorists So if you're the paranoid type, just put it away or leave it at home when going through airport security.

rating 5.0. Don't have to worry about protecting anymore This watch has been great for me. I have owned a lot of watches, but they have all tended to be fairly fancy and not meant for day to day wear. The only one that could really be worn day to day was my Moto 360 smartwatch. I found this Casio and fell in love with the design. The face is the perfect size and shape for me, because at 16 and 6' 1" I have extremely skinny wrists, and this watch isn't too big by any means. It is super simple to operate, and I would pay 2-3x the price for this watch. But as it is so cheap, I don't feel nervous about breaking it since it would be cheap to replace. So I don't pull any punches while wearing, per say. It's really nice to have a watch that I don't have to think about protecting.

Great buy.

rating 3.0. Good watch...poor watch band Don't get me wrong. Casio makes really good watches and sells them at a very low price. This watch, the F91W-1 is a great example of this....HOWEVER....they compromise drastically with the watch band. I have a very average size wrist but am forced to use the second to last hole in the band. By doing so, the keeper which you tuck the "remainder" of the band into is barely able to hold it. It would not cost much, if even a dollar to lengthen the bands on watches like the F91W-1. The F28-W is another one with short-band-itis. Because of the way the band is somewhat integrated into the design of the watch, changing/upgrading bands is not easy to do. Plus, removing and reinstalling the pins which secure the band to the watch is almost sure to damage the plastic tabs they mount in. Trust me, I've done it. One of Casio's best budget-priced watches, in my opinion, is the W201-1AV. Ample band length, shows day/month & date without pushing a button, PLUS a 10-year battery. Casio, please upgrade the bands on the other aforementioned watches among others.

rating 3.0. Skip this, get the FE10 I'm a guy of moderately above average size (six foot tall and stocky), and find the band length ungenerous (have to let the band almost all the way out). rating 5.0. i think the white rabbit was wearing one of these... this watch is EXACTLY what i have been looking for!
i use my watch at work all the time and have had several different ones over the last few years. while i really prefer a dial style watch, i find that the economy priced ones i have purchased tend to breal fairly quick. they dont make them like they used to. i decided to settle for a digital style watch and am so glad i got this one.
why do i like it so much?
1) its a simple design. i am pretty much fed up with this trend of watches weighing fifty pounds and having all sorts of blingy crap and dials all over the place. most of it is just for looks and when all is said and done, they just waste a lot of resources making a big hunk of junk to weigh your wrist down and get in the way. this one is fairly plain, and has the time and date clearly readable on the main screen. very straight forward without all the pseudo-tactical B.S. rambo junk.

2) it nice and SMALL. i can cover all the writing on the face of the watch with the end segment of my thumb (like making a thumb print on the screen) and it has a low,flat profile. that means you can put on a coat or shirt without it being a pain in the butt getting the watch through the sleeve.

3) its really light. you can feel your wearing it, but the sensation is subtle. wont bang around on your wrist joint and rattle you tendons like the big watches do.

4) its cheap! around 11 bucks, you cant go wrong.

5) water RESISTANT. (NOT water PROOF). this means that if you splash it while doing dishes, or get some rain on it, your not gonna have a dud at the end of the day. this is a really handy feature.

CONS:
really these are minor, but worth mention none the less.

1)the band is pretty small. it fits me fine but i have a thin wrist. i could see a person with a burly arm/wrist having a bad time with it. of course you can switch the band out, but you may not want to do that on a new watch. it fits 5.5 to 7.75 inch circumference wrist pretty comfortably, so you can check your wrist with a tape measure and see if it will work for you. the small size may be an issue for some.

2) apparently this is on the government list as a terrorist device used in making bombs or some crap. sounds like more paranoia to me, but it is a little creepie to think you could get flagged for being frugal.

thats really it on the cons. if you dont like simple watches, you wont like this. but if you want a nice practical watch that is really affordable and well made, this is a great choice. has a retro flavour too, which is fun. i hope to use this for a long time.
rating 5.0. Classic Great, simple watch. The band is a little stiff when new but loosens up pretty quickly. Nice design with a good light and simple controls.

There are two main design problems with this - one is the backlight, which sucks. It only comes from the left side. The second is the controls, they only gave it three buttons, but one is the backlight button. So when operating the watch in the dark you have to memorize how to do things like set an alarm, as the light button changes settings and the light does not stay on when you press it. So you have to press it once to see what screen you are on, then press the other buttons in the right combination to set the correct time for something like an alarm.

rating 5.0. Only reason we replace is due to a broken band, great quality for the price! These watches are great for the price. My husband has had 3 (well technically 4, but read below) over the last 8 years, and the only reason we replace is due to the band breaking. As I read on comments here, when the band breaks, for essentially the price of replacing the band, just buy a whole new watch, so that is what we do! There is no problem with the actual functions of the watch though. My hubby wears it all the time, during labor intensive jobs to swimming. Besides a turtle stealing it while scuba diving (the day after we purchased the first one it mysteriously disappeared while scuba diving), we haven't had a problem with one yet. We purchased a new one the next day and he did wear it scuba diving. He did not get mugged again it held up nicely. My husband does think the light on this one I bought off here is a touch more dim than usual though.
We love that we do not have to worry about breaking, losing, or being mugged by sea life because of low cost of these watches, but the quality far exceeds the price and they will forever be my husbands first choice in watches.

rating 5.0. The PERFECT Purse!!!!! I LOVE, LOVE this purse!!!!  It's just the right size with the perfect number of pockets.  I don't like purses with lots of compartments because it takes too long to find things.  I didn't realize until I got it, that it is made out of a very sturdy nylon type material, but you can't tell until you touch it.  It looks very classy!  The straps are wide enough so that they are very comfortable and don't dig into your shoulder.  Such a great price for a well made purse. I'm going to order a couple of other colors.
