input: rating 5.0. I won't pack without these. I won't travel without these cubes! It's like having a dresser in your suitcase. My family of four spent 2 weeks in New Zealand. Two people shared a suitcase. We each had a different color of packing cube set. I had my socks and undies in a medium back, shirts and pants in a large bag, hair car products in another, and swim suit, rash-guard, and towel in another. It was a great way to keep our suitcase organized. When we headed to the beach, I simply grabbed the right packing cube and away we went. They kept our suitcase well organized the entire trip AND kept the dirty clothes separate. I also believe you can fit a lot more into a suitcase when the clothes are in these cubes.  I won't pack a bag without them, long trip or short.  The cubes are very durable, with quality zippers.

rating 4.0. These were very nice and worked great These were very nice and worked great.  Good value for the money.  The quality was good, but not as high as others I have used.  Overall, good product.

rating 3.0. Adequate, but hardly inspirational Sizes seem rather odd, and the material is... flimsy. I don't know what my expectations were, so I don't know why I should feel disappointed. I think one is better off buying a set of multi-colored bags so the contents are obvious without unpacking. These work best when stuffed to attain some shape. The larger bags are good for carefully folded and layered dress shirts—but again, they should be packed in as tightly as possible to avoid squirming and wrinkles.

rating 3.0. even though sizes given in the product description, these ... even though sizes given in the product description, these were smaller than I had envisioned.  however, I've used once so far i was able to fit a fair amount of items in each and while I felt as if I was cramming the clothes in I didn't have wrinkling issues when I unpacked.  a few of the cube had loose threads from the zipper...only time will tell if this will become a problem.  for now I just cut them off.
rating 5.0. Neat and organized packing Don't know why I waited so long to buy these  . They are a great packing tool and I like the mesh Windows on them so you can see what's in each. Good price too

rating 4.0. It would be good if the cubes' material were stiffer to hold the ... The packing cube system arrived in a timely manner and well-packaged.  The cubes' fabric is strong, yet sufficiently pliable to accommodate over packing, and zippers are strong.  All shirts and trousers folded and placed in the cubes maintained their folds without additional wrinkling.  Cubes afforded the opportunity to pack clothes in combinations for a set # of days, which negated the need to open and unpack an entire suitcase. It would be good if the cubes' material were stiffer to hold the shape of the cube better; however, that might also disallow overpacking.  All-in-all, the TravelWise Packing Cube System was a good investment and will be used frequently for both long and short term travel.

rating 1.0. No. Just don't buy it. Seriously. One of the bags split at the seam while I was packing for the first use. I had NOT overstuffed. Disappointing. I had to sit and sew when I should have been packing. I do not recommend.

rating 5.0. Great if sharing a suitcase. We are very happy with these.  We were surprised at how much they held.  They are great when you have a family sharing a suitcase.  Each person can get a packing cube for clothes.  We recommend two sets of packing cubes for a family of 4-5.

rating 1.0. Cube ripped with first packing. I am incredibly disappointed. rating 5.0. Save space. I don't remember how I found out about this product, but I searched it on Amazon and decided to try it, since I was a few days away for my summer trip.
I red different reviews on how to pack my stuff. First I put away all the clothes I was taking for my 4 week trip and placed them in my suitcase as I used to do it before. Of course in a couple of minutes I didn't have space for my shoes or toiletry!. After this I rolled each piece of clothing and placed it inside of one of the bags and so on until it was full; I close the bag and placed it inside the suitcase. When I was done, I saw that half my suitcase was empty and now I have the space for the shoes.
I could easily fit the largest bag, 2 medium and 1 small. I took stuff that usually goes into 2 suitcases in only 1. I was so happy to save all this much space.
After coming back to the US, my cube system is still in perfect condition, no tears, no holes, no problem. I recommend it already to my friends; a life saver.
rating 2.0. Poor build quality The seems are coming undone after light use.  I like the idea of packing cubes but there are better options out there.

rating 5.0. A wise investment if you like to travel and/or organize your things in satisfying little shapes These packing cubes made me feel like I had my life together while I traveled Europe for a couple weeks. I don't, but they made me feel like I did.

I would suggest that TravelWise offer a three piece cube set. It would be nice to only pay for what you actually will use.

rating 5.0. Perfect bags for keeping your clothes Perfect bags for keeping your clothes, etc. organized while traveling!  I can easily fit more in my suitcase when I use these.

rating 5.0. Now that is amazing customer service both from the company and Amazon I purchased this set of five packing cubes selling at $22.95 along with the 3-piece set selling at $11.95 on Saturday, July 15 morning and received them both at 6 pm the SAME DAY! Now that is amazing customer service both from the company and Amazon. I bought these products from Travel/Wise based on other customer reviews and testimonials which are abundant on Amazon. I plan on using to organize my 21" carry-on luggage and pack more efficiently to travel lighter.

A little about the product. They are made very well out of a thicker nylon material with a double zipper that is not cheaply made. They are light weight and very malleable so they can be packed efficiently. They are not stiff or structured on the sides. That helps when packing as they will take on the shape and compress easily into the suitcase. I purchased for myself and fiance so I was maximizing the number of packing cubes by purchasing both sets. My trip is in two weeks and I can take a photo of the finished packing results at that time. But had to write a review about how economical these packing cubes as compared to others out there.

The pricing is fantastic for these pieces.

rating 5.0. Great buy, perfect for keeping families organized on trips! Excellent buy! My family of four took two separate trips this summer. On the first trip, we did not have the packing cubes. It was the usual stress of starting out organized and by the end of two weeks of living out of suitcases, the family was complaining- they couldn't find this or that, they could not fit everything back in the suitcase when repacking, etc. On our second summer trip, I bought a set of packing cubes for each family member and what a difference it made! We were totally able to find the thing we wanted, when we wanted it! Looking for pants? Get the pants pack? Time for bed? Get the pajama pack! It was awesome. And even when the kids didn't always put their stuff away, it was super easy for me to get things back in order quickly. Also, when we landed in a hotel for a few days, we just put our packs in the drawers. I bought a different color set for each family member and that made things a lot easier to keep track of, too. Repacking at the end was amazingly easy. It was a joy really! I have not washed them, I feel like they don't really need washing as we only kept clean clothes in them and they didn't get dirty otherwise. If I do feel they need a wash, I would simply hand wash them and hang them to dry, I can't imagine they wouldn't withstand that. Also, the zippers worked great, even when we overstuffed or carelessly zipped or unzipped. they are a good price and I highly recommend!

rating 3.0. One Star Damero is working on their product, and listening to their customers!
Upon leaving my first review after I bought the first purse and sent it back, Damero contacted me asking if I would be open to reviewing an improved version of the purse. I figured why not, I'll give it a shot.
The purse arrived within 5 days from their confirmation email. Very well packaged, wrapped in a protective foam, plastic bag and inside a sturdy box. The color is brown which is nice and warm, think milk chocolate brown.
Material is leather and it's obvious is a good quality that won't tear easily.
I am really impressed by how Damero as a company chooses to grow and build clientele and impove. I like that their focus is on accomodating the needs of their potential customers.
However the reason I give 3 stars is following:
The removable insert that comes with the purse, although good quality is much smaller than the purse. So you have this purse that is matching your expectations on the outside, and yet it gets cramped inside. Don't get me wrong, I think it's brilliant to have a removable liner that can be washed as we all know bottom of a purse can get quite gross. I just wish it had a better design. Plus, once the insert is removed, the purse does not have any zipper or any other way to close to stop pickpocketing or losing its contents.
If at there could be couple snap-on buttons added or something, that would be a step in the right direction.
Overall, I like the bag.

rating 5.0. First let me say that this necklace is beautiful and the picture doesn't even do it justice First let me say that this necklace is beautiful and the picture doesn't even do it justice. It sparkles like Austrial fire opals on steroids. Really breathtaking. Now comes the negative: Both the very low price and the fact that the metal type is only listed as "alloy" made me think. Well, follow this link[...] and find out for yourself that most Chinese jewelry is made of cadmium which is very toxic and cancer-causing. If the link doesn't work just google "Chinese jewelry, safety". As much as I dislike doing this it is going into the trash. Can't risk my health for this.

rating 4.0. Beautiful set I love this necklace set!  The colors are brighter than depicted, change as the light hits them, and appear to be plastic pieces that are semi-opaque.  The metal is very very cheap and would undoubtedly turn my neck green if I wore it too long, but appears somewhat sturdy, and I don't anticipate that the chain or links will break easily or that the plastic bits will pop out of their settings.  The earrings are light enough that I don't notice them.  And what great trickery!!  My husband wanted to know if they were labradorite!  ( I actually have a similar blue-green labradorite necklace and it is about 2x heavier) I wore this set out for our anniversary, with a low cut black dress and they were gorgeous!!  Will be wearing them often, for dress up nights out.  They also arrived a whole week before anticipated, which was much appreciated.

rating 3.0. The color I received was not purply-pink like this one--but more deep royal blue-green with opalescent touches--which ... This necklace/earring set is dramatic!  The color I received was not purply-pink like this one--but more deep royal blue-green with opalescent touches--which was fine for me but if you're trying to match it to a particular outfit that might be a problem.  If you look closely at the necklace, it sort of looks like crumpled tinfoil under colored plastic, but this really can't be seen unless you are holding it in your hand and really staring at it.  I wore my necklace for a New Year's Even party with a plain black dress and recieved many compliments on my "outfit" although dress itself is quite boring and basic, this necklace seemed to turn it into a showstopper! One problem I had though was my semi-sensitive skin became irritated when I wore it once with perfume--I believe the combination resulted in sort of a chemical reaction.  It went away once I removed the necklace, so I'm pretty sure the necklace was the cause.  I left the perfume off my chest the next time and had no problems.  If you like dramatic, statement pieces, this ones worth the small investment!

rating 5.0. Lovely, shimmering necklace set! So pretty. Changes color as it catches the light. The chameleon effect is my favorite aspect of the necklace. Husband loved it too. Mine looks more green dominant than pink, which I prefer. It's a good weight and doesn't look cheap or gaudy like a lot of costume jewelry can. I am wearing the earrings on their own right now and they feel light and non-irritating. A good purchase! Took 17 days to get to me in CA. Worth the wait, especially for the price!

rating 5.0. great for night driving. I have had trouble driving at night for about 5 years now and was previously wearing amber lenses to cut down on the brightness...but that, in turn, darkened everything and I still couldn't drive more than 30 minutes at night.  I'm only in my 30's so this is a problem!  Our closest family members live a couple hours away so trips began to get cut very short since I always had to leave early to get home before dark. I bought these glasses last year and I absolutely love them! They brighten everything and cut the glare of lights so I can drive longer and see better at night.  I can drive almost 2 hours at night now, so shopping trips and family visits are much more frequent.  These glasses have been a blessing and I highly recommend them if you have trouble driving at night.  I am thinking of ordering a backup pair for the glove box, and a separate pair to keep in my husband's truck.

rating 5.0. Great build quality for what you pay. Let's face it, if your like me and break/lose sunglasses every month, then investing in Ray-Bans or the like is probably not a viable solution. So, I went on a shopping spree for "cheap" sunglasses on Amazon (<$10), and these were one of the pairs I purchased. I was pleasantly surprised!

PROS:

-Inexpensive!

-Perfect size for medium/large heads.

-Look fantastic.

-Build quality is actually not bad at all for what you pay.

-Well-packed

CONS:

-The anti-glare hue is actually more orange than amber, and so a bit brighter than I expected.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Great for night driving I love these for driving at night. They cut down on the intensity of approaching headlights so I no longer feel a stabbing pain. They "flatten out" the bright lights and "dull" them so they're no longer piercing. If you have trouble with headlights in your eyes at night and find all those LED and super-bright headlights painful when they come at you, then these are the glasses for you.

rating 5.0. Perfect!! I get so many complements o. This bag. I did have to wash off a little glue and cut some threads, but for the price of such a great quality back, I didn't mind it at all. It is such an amazing deal! If you're hesitating, definitely get it now!

rating 3.0. Not as pictured This bag IS truly unique, though don't let the photos fool you. They do warn you in the description of the natural defects in the leather, but what they don't tell you is that the purse will be discolored (and I mean REALLY discolored---like 5-6 shades lighter in some places) and patched from leathers of varying quality, bordering on Frankenstein. When I got it, the bag felt so dry and the shoulder strap so frail/aged that I was almost afraid to wear it. Hoped that some mink oil would rejuvenate it but could only do so much as the strap is in such bad shape. There were also sections with stray pieces of animal hair still attached to it, but that may just be unavoidable due to the handmade craftsmanship.

At the same time, however, I love the overall design of the bag. Great pockets and all of the zippers, snaps, and buckles are high quality. In fact, the bottom portion of the bag was made MUCH better that shoulder strap. Even the interior is nice and has a much darker green and higher quality fabric than pictured. The smell wasn't that strong when I first got it and went away fairly quickly. Probably would've kept it if it weren't for the shoddy strap, inconsistent color, and patches of stray hair.

rating 5.0. Nice leather and bag style for a great price For a few years I've been looking for nice leather bags comparable to the Fossil brand, and I think this comes the closest for its price. The size is perfect with just enough convenient storage pockets. The strap is a little bit long if you're not wearing it cross-body, and the front latch is a little difficult to open/close because it's a belt and buckle closure. I love the style and the leather is nice, and I get a lot of compliments! Would recommend!

Once I had them perfectly fitted to my teeth I used the wax to mold them in place. Make sure you do not over heat the wax but def get it hot enough to be moveable. Hold them in place for about a minute and a half.
It's super hard to talk in them and I'm sure you couldn't eat or drink with them but for a Halloween costume and the price they are perfect. If you are looking to wear them on a regular basis I would spend the money and get nice ones.

rating 5.0. A Nice Option for Women Who Don't Have Pierced Ears This thing is TINY!

That's not a criticism, just something to be aware of. I think the photo of it on a woman's ear is a bit misleading. Either that or it is a child's ear. Otherwise... it is nicely made, with nice detail.

Its small size presents one minor issue the first few times you put it on. When you hold it in your hand you really cannot see how you are trying to fit it to your ear. Your hand completely hides the cuff, making it trial and error guess work. However, with practice that issue goes away.

This was a surprise gift for my wife. Her ears are not pierced severely limiting her choice of earrings. Her tastes tend to be conservative, so this was a risky purchase. Fortunately... she like it very much and has since bought other cuff style earrings because of this one.

rating 1.0. Not even 1 star... My fault for not reading the reviews before purchasing. To begin with, I did not even attempt to put these in my nose as the way they were packaged was awful. It was a piece of clear packing tape with the rings stuck to it and folded over. Not sanitary at all. (Yes I know how to sanitize but it's the point)
False advertising at it's finest, no way this would fit a 22 or 20 guage nose piercing without stretching the hole. Not interested in having a huge hole in my nose.
The rings I received were the blue and the rainbow, that was fine with me, however upon having to pick them off the tape (I actually was going to use them in my belly piercing) THE COLOR CAME OFF ON THE TAPE!!! So I had a half rainbow/half silver colored one and the color on the blue one came off the ball. I have pictures but the review won't let me post them.
Overall, cheap price. Guess you get what you pay for is true in this case.

input: rating 5.0. BEST WATCH EVER This is an awesome watch. It's great for anime fans which is the reason i bought it, but it's also an extremely cute necklace.I made all my anime friends jealous with this awesome death note watch. Plus, it's super cheap great buy!

rating 3.0. Very cool necklace I really love the concept of this necklace. Since I am diehard Death Note fan, this was something I just HAD to have. The combination of a necklace and a watch together is really cool.

I was happy to have received this item so quickly. The seller "animemart" had packaged in in a little zip-lock bag. When I opened it, I noticed that the front was scratched a lot, but not so much to be noticeable (you'd have to really look to see it). Also, the inside of the necklace seemed to have a large black border at the top of the watch part which appears to look like a gradient of some sort, as it starts to get smaller at the right side (kind of hard to explain, looks like a mess up in the painting or something). The opening to the necklace on the inside has a lot of black marks also. Basically, it does not look as good quality as it does on the preview picture on Amazon.

Overall, the necklace is really cool, but I wish it was a little bit in better condition, although it was described as "new" it looked like it was used a lot, if not just handled poorly. The watch part works great and it is a stylish necklace to wear, I recommend it to any DN fan.
rating 5.0. Great It's really awesome! I'm glad I got it. Came in great condition. It's black on the outside and white on the inside.
rating 2.0. Death Note Notebook L necklace watch The clock doesn't keep its set time. I have reset it several times, but it won't hold. So it looks cool, but is useless...

rating 4.0. Nice Necklace! The chain is different from the one in the image- a bit short too, but it's still great. The whole necklace is pretty heavy. The front cover of the Death Note doesn't stay open by itself, you have hold it open or the hinges will keep it shut. There weren't any scratches on the front of it, but there were some scratches along the inside of the note where the hinges close the front cover with the rest of it. Shipping was good; it arrived in about two weeks.

rating 5.0. it's perfect The book part itself is so cute! Plus, the clock actually works. It is actually also pretty solid, and the hinges of the book are nice and close by itself (so if you don't have to worry about closing it) One thing is that the necklace part is too short; however, you can easily put another string or necklace chain through the book so that it fits around your head.
This is a perfect present and I am very happy. I am definitely going to buy this again, or buy it again for myself.

rating 2.0. It looks like someone had this before me. Okay let me start off by saying, you get what you pay for I suppose. I ordered a new one on April 11th and got it on the 18th. Shipping was very quick and I was pleased with that. However, when I opened the package and looked at the notebook watch itself, I noticed quite a few things. First, It was dirty. As if someone had used this before me. Secondly, it had marks on it that didn't come off, like a smear stain. Lastly, the white in "Death Note" wasn't filled in completely. Now upon opening it I saw it had more dirt inside that's very difficult to remove. I noticed the clock was wrong so I attempted to fix it. But, when I was turning the little adjuster, it wasn't doing a thing. The watch still moves and tells time, but you can't adjust it. I am very unhappy.

rating 4.0. Way nicer than expected For the price, I was expecting a little plastic piece of crap with a non-functional clock. I didn't really care though, because I love Death Note and figured it was a cute charm even if the timekeeper didn't work (and honestly, who checks their necklace for the time).  What I got was a nice, heavy charm. Fully functional. Feels pretty well constructed even.  The chain is an absolute piece of garbage, but I just replaced it with a better one, and now I have an awesome piece of Death Note jewelry.

rating 4.0. Pretty good First off, the shipping was really fast, I got it in about a week and a half. It seems very solid and is working at the moment at least, good for the money; the only reason I didn't give it a full review is because the front is a bit smeared, it's a tad scratched but it looks like the paint wasn't fully allowed to dry and so it's a bit miscolored. Still, I don't think my brother will mind too much; for what I paid it's great.

rating 4.0. This necklace is pretty cool To be honest, I'm not a huge fan of Death Note, in fact I rarely watched it when I was younger. But seeing the necklace in the picture given it looked pretty cool and since I collect necklaces, I had to have it for myself. It took a while to come in, but eventually, I got it. The product was not all that fantastic like I had hoped.Minus some scratching off the painting on the book's sides, it came in good quality. The book is bigger than I had imagined but the clock works so I have no complaints. Seeing as how the price very cheap for me, I say it's worth it to buy this necklace, especially if you're a fan.

rating 4.0. Perfect For Death Note Fans! I bought this a long time ago and got it yesterday. I thought I had got ripped off. But when I got it, it was perfect! Except for the letters. The letters on the cover of the note are deeply fadded. Unless you put it up right in front of your eyes you can't tell it says Death Note...Other than that I love it and so will you!

rating 4.0. This is a really awesome little necklace for the anime lover in your life This is a really awesome little necklace for the anime lover in your life. It is definitely something that you do not see a lot. Great item at a great price! I love the long chain as well!

rating 4.0. A Neat Pocket Watch! This is really cool necklace for Death Note fans! I only have a few little issues with it. Firstly, it's a lot smaller than I expected. A rounded measurement puts this pocket watch's measurements at about 1.5 inx1 in, only a little larger than a quarter. Secondly, the chain that comes with the watch is ridiculously long. The chain hits only a few inches above my belly button, but it's not difficult to take the charm off of it and put it on a better chain. Finally, there's the ticking. I've never had a pocket watch before, so I don't know if it's normal or not, but this watch is pretty loud. Not intolerable, but in a quiet room people who are near you may hear it. Other than that though I love it and for the price these are definitely just minor complaints.

If your a Death Note fan or know one your going to be a hit with this watch. Its fun to get a complement on the necklace and then open it up to a watch and everyone gos "Woah how cool"!!!

rating 3.0. These are all one bracelet, can not separate! I thought this would be a cute set of bracelets to wear with summer outfits.  I guess this is my fault for not paying closer attention to the ad,  but I was disappointed when these came and they are all attached in one piece so they can not be worn separately.  Because of the metal piece holding them together for the chain and clasp, they bunch up and do not space out nicely as shown in the photograph in the ad.  They overlap each other so it doesn't look as nice when you wear them.  I also found that from being packaged the leather bracelets have creases in them from being folded that don't seem to be going away.  The price is inexpensive so I wouldn't bother returning the item, but I was hoping it would look more like the ad and not a squashed pile on my wrist.

rating 5.0. Does the trick! I bought this because healer friend advised that rose quartz might counteract ill effects from using a fitbit - I was inexplicably exhausted, which stopped as soon as I stopped using the fitbit. As my friend said, in some people such devices that constantly emit EMFs can reverse the body's polarity and wreak havoc (un-believers, no comments please!). So she muscle tested me, and found that holding the fitbit alone, my response became weak. Holding rose quartz as well, my response was strong.

Well, this bracelet did the trick! I can wear my fitbit now with no problem, with the bracelet next to it - though no longer wear it at night. It's also pretty enough that I wear it at other times without the fitbit. Can't argue with the price, either.

rating 4.0. Dainty, cute, shinny, sturdy and that too at a good price!! Packaging: I found a tinny plastic bag in my mailbox today and it took me a minute to understand that the seller did actually put the product inside. Lol. The earrings came in tinny cover(Picture attached).
About the product: These are about 11 mm in diameter (around 1/2 an inch). Once I wiped the earring with a muslin cloth they were shinning like expensive earrings. The earring has a click-to-close mechanism which is strong enough. Hence once you wear it I can assure that it won't open unless you attempt to remove the earrings. These are dainty and cute and fit perfectly on the ear. I can assure that these can be worn by adults as well as teenage girls. For the price I paid these are really cute and sturdy. I will update the review if I see any change in the material or functionality of the earrings. For now I am just going to show - off :)
Pros:
Shinny
Cute
Can be worn for both professional and social events
Nice price ( I paid $3.30 including shipping for these)

Cons:
I do not see any

I gave 4 stars because there is scope of improvement in the packaging. My earrings make some clinking sound at times and I sincerely hope it is not because of some damage to the product in the transit. However these are nice earrings and a good purchase.

rating 5.0. I received shipment promptly and the ring arrived in great shape along with a satin pouch I received shipment promptly and the ring arrived in great shape along with a satin pouch. The pouch was not necessarily my "cup of tea" but it certainly suits its purpose. The ring is attractive and definitely has a handmade feel about it. To negate the "green finger" comments, I sprayed mine (both inside and outside) with a clear, semi-gloss enamel spray. (Rustoleum brand). The semi-gloss enamel really made the detail pop. In fact, (in my opinion) the ring is more attractive after applying the spray. I let it dry in the bright sunshine for about an hour then I set it in front of a fan for another hour. No tackiness or stickiness. Looks great. Recommended, especially after spraying with clear, semi-gloss enamel.

Although this item doesn't specify a size, as a one-size-fits all for swimsuit coverup, it came packaged with a sticker stating it was size Large. Personally, I find it runs a bit oversized, although the material is very comfortable and lightweight. I plan to use more so as a comfortable loungewear during vacation in a hot climate.
The color is also slightly different than the description photo as the blues are slightly more muted. The seams are nicely completed though and the sleeves are a batwing style. I am 5'7" and the length reaches down to my ankles.
Overall, a nice casual coverup and multi-use item. This can easily be thrown into a suitcase without worry about taking up space or wrinkling.
Item came to me shipped earlier than expected which was appreciated.
This item was purchased at a discounted rate in exchange for my unbiased opinion and review.

rating 3.0. No shape It's pretty, but has a very strange fit.  It's wide and flat- no shape, just two rectangle pieces of fabric sewn together at the seams. I think I could have made this myself! rating 5.0. Super cute for the pool! I am reviewing the Akery Women's Chiffon Robe Beach Coverup.  First off, let me say, the print is so cute!  It has a tribal feel to it that I just love.  The dark and light blues and oranges are great together.  The product looks just like the picture shows, so no surprise there.  The construction and design is super simple, but sewn together nicely.  The edges have been serged on the sides and folded over and stitched around the neckline, which leads to its comfort -- no scratchy edges.  Not sure what the fabric is, but reminds me of a chiffon - lightweight and sheer throughout.  This will be so cute to throw on as a coverup to run to the pool or beach.  I am quite tall and do have larger hips, so it did get a little tight on the upper thigh and over the booty, but I think it is still ok to throw on for the pool.  It looks fantastic on my tall daughter with her athletic build.  Girly, feminine with a bit of funk - I really like this.  *Disclaimer, I received this product at a discounted price for my honest review.

rating 2.0. like silverware. And it's a shame because it's an ... I knew the minute I opened the package that this was not solid sterling silver--the dark color of it and high shine...it has the color of stainless steel, like silverware. And it's a shame because it's an adorable, petite little bracelet and I love the design, however it is not sterling silver. The joining pieces near the clasp are turning gold, and I've worn it maybe 5-7 days straight now. The pictures show a nice strong-looking lobster clasp, and it's just a little flimsy, very thin circular clasp that is hard as the devil to fasten, and is so thin it's only a matter of time til it pops and falls to the ground, and the person who finds it and picks it up will say, "Oh, it's just a fake cheap little bracelet." and will throw it back down. Can't be that upset b/c of the price, only 5.00, but I would have paid more if it were solid sterling silver, as advertised...which is most definitely false. Design is adorable! Quality is a joke.

rating 2.0. Very disappointed Very disappointed,  Im a 36 34 40, 5'4" 145 lbs. I  usually wear a medium  but on amazon I usually have to by a large or xl, so i went with a large. This dress was so cute in the picture, but does not fit like picture depicts. I did like it but saw to many cons to keep it. First off it was not the type of material i hoped it would be, i wanted material with minimal stretch. Second, it was much longer than the picture depicted, I wanted one that fell above the knee and this one was 2 inches below the knee plus the cut for the hips was at my upper thigh making it look odd.Third, the bottom hemline was crooked, im a bit ocd and that would drive me up a wall. Forth, the size chart is very confusing, with the way they have it listed it seems like all sizes should fit. I was thinking about trying it in a smaller size but i would have to get a small to fit the length I want and im afraid that would be to tight around my waist and not hide my belly fat. rating 4.0. Slim, soft, functional, great for traveling, just not big enough for every day use The only reason I didn't give it a 5 is because as most women do, I carry a lot of cards - not CC, but ID - insurance, AAA, etc.  So I had to really cut back on what I carried in this.  But when I travel, I don't need the cards I don't use often. I keep in a metal RIFD-safe mini-wallet, so I have them on hand in my purse.  But when traveling, I leave those cards at home anyway.... things like Costco card, movie membership card, restaurant cards, etc. This wallet is nice to the touch, compact, and actually holds my iPhone 5S, passport, cash, and credit cards.  I really like the fact that it has a zippered coin section. I liked it so much I bought two more for my friends who travel with me.

rating 5.0. Great wallet - durable, colorful, practical I really like this wallet. It's streamlined and elegant, with a very simple, unadorned design. I like that one of the billfold pockets can actually hold my Iphone 6, so it's perfect for when I just want to run into a store and carry my wallet and tuck my phone inside it. That same billfold slot/pocket is also big enough for your passport, as pictured. If you're one of those people who carries 20 credit cards & a ton of bills & change and coffee cards with you, this wallet won't work, it does have limited space. But if you want to pare down, it's great.

rating 5.0. Sleek and well-made, awesome price! I travelled to the Dominican Republic with my teenage son this summer, and as it was just the two of us, simple and easy was priority #1. I chose the red because I didn't want to dig for it in my black bag, and when I set it on a counter (airline, immigration, etc.) it was unforgettable (to someone who forgets things!). My only issue is really a nonissue:  I wish it had a tiny zippered compartment, even on one of the existing pockets. I can deal with a lot of cash, but it would've been nice to have a small spot for coins. But it held cash, credit cards, boarding passes, and two standard-issue (noon-extension) passports with ease.

rating 5.0. Very Effective Easily removes tarnish and restores shine and luster to jewelry, particularly costume.  I've used it on several pieces of jewelry, and have found it to be very effective. rating 5.0. Awesome but don't use on pearl, ivory, opal, coral/soft stones Like the other reviewers said- this is a handy product. Folds up small, really gets the tarnish off my sterling silver jewelry.

I wish someone had given me one of these as a wedding or birthday present many years ago!

Didn't realize when I bought it that it doesn't work on all jewelry. The note that came with it says "Cleans, Shines & Protects Gold, Silver and Platinum. Caution: do not use on 24 karat gold, ivory, pearls, opals, coral, lapis, malachite and other soft or porous stones".

Not sure what kind of chemicals are in the cloth itself- I noticed a chemical smell lingered on one of the necklace chains I cleaned with it-- I think more polishing with the outer fabric will do the trick (there is an inner and outer fabric- inside for cleaning and outside for polishing). Suggest washing your hands carefully after handling and don't let kids play with this. (I had previously tried the "natural" cleaners using baking soda and aluminum foil to no avail)

Otherwise very effective and makes my jewelry look great again.

rating 5.0. I wasn't convinced by the reviews Sometimes, when I look up a product online and the reviews are all raving about it, I figure that the reviews are mostly from people who work for the company or something like that. Honestly, this product had that ring to me - the reviews were all so positive, I thought for sure they were all fake. I bought it anyway because I'm very comfortable with Amazon customer service & return policy.

I had some jewelry that needed polishing. Some is sterling silver dipped in rhodium, some is gold. I didn't want to buy many different cleaners and thought that this might have a chance to be a one stop shop.

The day it came, I pulled it out and laughed - it looked like just a regular microfiber dust rag. Then I used it. In a matter of 10 seconds, the ring I used it on was shiny again. Wow. I was really excited by that. Then I tried it on a few other pieces - all were sparkling in a matter of seconds. I was seriously amazed.

So. Whenever I get really excited about a product, I think about all the other ways that I might be able to use it. Then it hit me. Last week was my anniversary with my husband and we drank a glass of champagne out of the toasting flutes from our wedding to celebrate. My husband washed them afterward but didn't dry them... The stems are made from silver. When I woke up in the morning, I found our toasting flutes to be horrifying - I actually cried. I thought they were ruined forever because the silver was all yellowed and horrible.

This morning, I got the idea to try this rag out on the stems. I kid you not, in about 2 minutes of scrubbing with the rag (they were horrible), my toasting flutes looked almost brand new again. There are a few spots that probably wont be able to be fixed because it seems like the silver kind of got almost burnt off.. but seriously, the glasses are silver again and they're beautiful. The rag on the other hand.. it's totally black from this big project. But for only a few dollars, I'll happily buy another one to use on my jewely. For only a few dollars, this cloth saved my irreplacable toasting flutes.

If you're looking for something to polish your jewelry, this is a great, perfect product - I'll be using it forever. If you're now thinking about all the other things you can use it to polish, I do suggest buying a few clothes at the same time because big projects ruin it kind of fast. However, it was worth every single penny. I couldn't be happier.

input:
rating 3.0. Prepare for numerous frustrations. Let me start by saying that unless you are in the 1% of the population that has the perfect shaped head for this mask, you're going to have issues with it and need to modify it. The space for the head is huge. I can only assume it was designed that way to accommodate for girls with really long and voluminous hair. If you have short hair, you should plan ahead to have to pad the inside or else it will dent in. I used Kleenex with a wig cap.

The chin strap on this thing is awkwardly made. It should have been designed to fit under the chin, but instead it is manufactured to rest on the chin. If you try to put it under your chin, the mask will crease awkwardly. I ended up cutting it off, which of course then created the problem that the mask did not want to stay on.

You will sweat if you wear this mask for any decent length of time. Not surprisingly, when you sweat, it will slide up on your face. My biggest battle with this mask was keeping it in place. I was constantly having to pull it down.

The back of the mask has a slit down the middle so that it will fit over your head but no way to keep the slit closed once the mask is on. If you don't want it flapped open awkwardly, you will have to install some type of closure. I used velcro, which I don't recommend because it didn't want to stay a lot of the time. If I had to do it over again(which I might next year), I would use a hook and eye closure system.

Also, the back of the mask does not go down all the way onto your neck. The bottom of your hairline will show. Depending on which version of Cat Woman you're doing, this is fine. Since I was going for the Michelle Pfeiffer look, it was a bit irritating.

I have to give this mask 3 stars just for existing. If you're into cosplay, it will save you a lot of time designing your own mask, but please know that you will still have to work with it a bit.

rating 5.0. Awesome mask! Only a few issues Awesome mask! Only a few issues...the chin strap folds in an awkward way while on (it's not really a big deal, that can be fixed). Since there is a slit cut into the back (to aide in putting it on, I presume?), I'll probably be installing some snaps or velcro. The strips below the eyes fit a bit snugly to your face, but other than that, the mask fits comfortably...certainly not too tight. All in all, I am quite pleased with this mask, any problems I have found can be taken care of; it looks great and is very true to the look of the character.

rating 1.0. This has a horrible fit. Even though it has a slit in ... This has a horrible fit. Even though it has a slit in the back, it doesn't fit my head properly. I have a normal sized head, and have never had problems with masks before. This fits so tight, it makes your skin bulge. It also gaps where it isn't skin tight. Love the design, but it's pointless because of the horrible fit. It also doesn't lay around the nose correctly. Would never recommend this mask.

I am doing a distressed look so I am making more cuts in it which makes it more comfortable to wear. This mask definitely looks better if you work on it. The stitches are painted on with grey paint and the eye sockets were way too small compared to the mask in the movie. I painted the stitches white and fixed the eye sockets as it looks a lot better. Didn't like how it has the DC COMICS on the back of the mask instead of the inside of it. There's a opening on the back but I think i'll just sew it closed and make extra stitches. Like I said, it's not bad. It's a good working base, but you just have to make it better.

rating 5.0. Love it. Fit well. This mask has great potential for my puposes. It is sturdy and solid. Mine had no air bubbles in the cast and all of the stiches are molded nicely and well painted. However my plan is to scrape off the stitches as I am going as comic cat woman and not Michelle Pfeiffer catwoman.
this mask is definitely molded for one size of head. I'm not sure how to measure this, but if you are in doubt about whether or not it will fit, it fits me pretty well, so here are some of my measurements:

Circumference, parallel to the ground, around my forehead:
21.5"

Circumference round my face, in front of the ears, to under my chin:
22.5"

I hope that helps at all.

The chin strap on this mask is strange though. It is as if the stap is supposed to go on your chin as opposed to under it.
the angle of it is weird, and because of this I might cut it off.
The left eye hole I'm this mask seems larger than the right.
This mask has a keyhole cut into the back, so that you can get it on. My plan is to put holes in this so that I can lace it shut, but it doesn't gape open on my head or anything.
also molded into the back of the cowl are the words "MADE IN CHINA" and "C 2014 DC COMICS Inc"
They easily could have put this on the inside. But it not terribly obvious or ugly.

Over all this is a very good mask. There isn't another one like it.

rating 1.0. don't be like me, use your common sense I knew when I made this purchase, that magnets stick to metal, and my face is not made of metal. However, when I saw these fake "magnetic nose ring studs" I thought to myself, "I'm pretty sure magnets only stick to metal or other magnets, and my nose is neither of those things. But - people are buying these, so they must work somewhat, right? Maybe I won't be able to wear them all day, but this could be a good way to figure out if I want to get my nose pierced, for real." I ignored the three star rating, and went ahead and bought them.

When I opened up the package I was happy to see an array of beautiful colors, and studs that are even smaller than they look in the picture. Perfect for me, because if I get a nose stud, I want it to be on the smaller side. I cut the package open, picked out an earring, pressed it to my nose, and guess what happened? exactly what you would expect to happen if you stick a magnet to your face. it fell to the floor, and bounced around the hard wood, slipping into a crack somewhere never to be seen again. Luckily, there are 24 magnetic nose studs in the set, giving me ample opportunity to try again. I did try again, and the same thing happened - again and again. Standing there like a fool, trying to get a magnet to stick to my skin, by pressing it to my face as hard as I could, it dawned on me that maybe in the future I shouldn't ignore obvious red flags when making any purchase.

Learn from my mistake. Don't buy these.

input:
rating 5.0. Essentially a PRW-3000 on Steroids I've a had a few Casio watches including some from the ProTrek line. While the PRW-5000 is my favourite looking ProTrek....it is also the most annoying to work ProTrek with the analogue.....and that mini digital display is just brutal.....it looked awesome but in my opinion a ProTrek should be mostly digital. I liked the module of the PRW-3000 but it lacked that rugged mountain man look that its predecessors had....a beautiful watch.....but lacking that metal and rugged appeal that I personally enjoyed from the series.

Enter the PRW-3500. Same guts as the PRW-3000 but with a stainless steel ring and dive rated. The watch is big but not overwhelming.....I have 7" wrists and it does not look out of place or overly massive. The buttons are well placed and easily accessed......if you know ProTreks the functions are all there. Altimeter, Barometer, Compass, LED nightlight, solar, sunset/sunrise data, timers, alarms, etc...etc...etc. The watch is Multi-Band 6 and syncs excellently on the West Coast.

This watch is great.....get some!!!!

rating 5.0. Excellent choice, updated sensors for accuracy and size Does everything I was looking for. Came pretty quickly and was packaged securely. My only complaint is the standard Protrek box and packaging. You're spending quite a bit on these watches and it'd be nice if they came with a good looking box like the G-Shocks. BUT it appeals to the conservationist in me. The whole box is your traditional cardboard with some green Protrek labels and designs. Inside that is the manual, warranty card, and a little guide to receive the radio control signal and the Protrek box. The box is sort of cool. Looks like it's made from pressed recycled material. There is some green cardboard inside the wooden\recycled box holding the watch in place with a cheap plastic bag over it. The face and back are covered with some stick-on thick plastic like your cellphone came with. Manual is very large but it offers a lot of different languages and can be sort of confusing at first. I'd say about 1\8 of it is English. If you are using this watch for the field I'd say be sure to pack that up as well. There's a lot of functions and ways to operate different things so you should be prepared.

Receiving the atomic radio control sync can be a pain inside my house just outside of Chicago. Put it by the window facing west?(Towards Colorado Radio tower) doesn't sync. Put it on the floor next to my bed and wall facing West. It syncs. I'm sure out in the field, away from the city and interference I'm positive this will sync up with out a hitch.

Compass gives you the directional reading two ways. One is a degree heading (180 Degrees South) the 12 o'clock is the heading. and there are 4 dots that spin around for N S E W. North is 3 dots.

Aside from that you will want to calibrate the altimeter, barometer and thermometer if you're truly interested in accuracy like I am. I'd say mine from factory was about -\+7 degrees, meters and InHg/HaPa. Pretty accurate for a small solar panel watch.

On the solar side mine came half charged. Took about 2 or so in the window on a sunny day to charge up fully and hasn't dropped down since. Power save mode disables the display when it's completely dark for a while so if you wake up and it's blank just press a button and it'll wake up. Seems like a very sturdy and well built watch and looks to me like it'll last for quite some time. Pretty happy with the purchase, wish I could have gotten the Orange model myself but I'm not willing to shell out extra just for a different color.

rating 5.0. Great watch Great watch. I had the old version non-solar and liked it except for replacing batteries. This version being solar should eliminate that problem.  I got the one with metal bezel (my old was with black) - this looks slightly larger, but in actual fact smaller. Compass is different than previous version. Overall love it and would recommend.

rating 5.0. 2 thumbs up Very nice watch. If you like big watches this is a good choice. I have a 9" wrist so I gravitate to the bigger watches anyway. This is my 4th Pro Tek and they have all been great. I like these because of the big digits. I don't have to put my reading glasses on to see the time. I really don't use the ABC functions that much but the few times I have they were fairly accurate. Love the atomic feature. All my Casios have it. It's nice to know my watch is always accurate down to the second. I got a great price at 164.00 dollars but it went up right after I bought mine. Can't find it at that price now.

rating 5.0. Great New Pathfinder! I am a big fan of Casio G Shocks and Pathfinders--have a big good number of each.  Last year I bought several of the PRG 270 models--really do like them and became one of my favorite models.  I liked the new version of the sensors and really liked the large display.  I am also a fan of atomic timekeeping.  When the new 3500 model came out w/solar and atomic timekeeping, I knew I would want to add one to my collection.  The new model has become my favorite watch--at least for now.  The sensors work fine--if you know how to properly use them--which means calibration at times.  I liked the added "toughness" of this model--although I never had a problem with the PRG 270 or any other  of the Pathfinder models for that matter.  It is a pretty big watch so some may not be able to wear it--I have an 8" wrist and have no problems.  It has basically the same features as the PRG 270 but with the added atomic timekeeping and 200m water resistance.  It is--for me--a comfortable watch to wear.  So far, I am very satisfied.

rating 5.0. ... have a 7 inch wrist and this watch looks great. It looks durable I have a 7 inch wrist and this watch looks great.  It looks durable.  I have always had a Casio for daily wear and this fits the bill.  It will go under my shirtsleeve but only barely - obviously not to be worn with a shirt and suit.  It is very easy to read even in bright daylight.  As usual the method for adjustment of the various functions has a learning curve to it, but I was able to figure it out without the manual.

rating 4.0. Excellent qualitywatch for out door and looks good also Excellent quality watch for out door and looks good also.
loved it.

rating 5.0. Excellent Full Featured Watch At first I was a little concerned with the display size, compared to my older watch PAG-50 Pathfinder (I had that one for 9 years). After using it for a week, I love it. The atomic time keeping, world time, stopwatch, countdown timer, water proof up to 200m, and the updated sensor is a big plus. The band is comfortable and has plenty of adjustment. This watch is not overwhelming in size and fits comfortably. I wish it showed the phases of the moon and tides, but was not a deal breaker for me. If it was up to me I would have liked to see a little bit of a different format on the display, such as the placement of the date, and seconds. If you like big watches with a lot of functionality, then this is a great watch. I am really happy with my purchase.
rating 4.0. Four Stars Really like the watch; rugged, functional and good looking.

rating 2.0. Disappointing Disappointing!  After receiving the watch, reading the manual, and setting the city and Day Light Savings Time.  The auto setting for Day Light Savings time did not work.  The watch also was turned toward the west sun for 4 to 5 hours for two straight days, yet it never left M (medium power setting) designation for the solar battery.  It should have moved by the end of the first day to H for high battery power.  I returned the watch to Amazon, and received a full refunded of the purchase price.

rating 5.0. Casio Men's PRW-3500-1CR Atomic Resin Digital Watch Great watch!  This watch replaces an earlier version and has greater accuracy when taking altimeter readings. rating 3.0. A full-featured watch for outdoors, but not as sturdy as you might think :( I was very excited to get this watch. It has many features including barometer, thermometer, altimeter (based on barometer) and electronic compass along with typical features found in digital watches. It is solar powered too!
My first concern before getting this watch was the size. I thought it might be too big for my wrist. It isn't. It is a big watch, but it looks great.

I bought this watch to use out doors while hiking, camping and climbing mountains; I didn't want to worry about it getting scratched --it's not exactly cheap! (Yes, mine already has a scratch from my dog.)

rating 4.0. Great watch, awesome price, buy it with Casio original manufacturer's warranty! My husband looked high and low for a great watch with few characteristics. It had to be solar powered, with countdown timer, stopwatch and alarm, plus it had to be a light watch to wear it daily for work. He was super excited to find this watch on Amazon. It has tons of other features that he, as outdoors man, also considers great advantages. It is a bigger watch, but not enormous like some are. He loves how light it feels on his wrist. It is quite easy to see the time, but smaller symbols and numbers require him to wear glasses, and he uses +1.5 glasses to read. The only downfall of this watch is that if you have lots of hair on your arm and near the wrist, it might be pulling your hair as the inside of the band has grooves. It might be fixed by adjusting the band to the length that you can work with or get used to. One last advice - buy the watch that has original Casio warranty from Amazon (you can't beat their price now anywhere else, and this is quite a new model), and the register it on Casio website for warranty. We did it and Casio gave us 2nd year warranty for free with condition that this watch was not bought from a third party seller.

rating 5.0. Beefy, easy to read, feature-packed My response to the rise of smart watches has been a desire to go retro, and when it comes to retro digital chic, nobody beats Casio.

My first purchase was a [[ASIN:B000GAWSDG Casio F91W-1 Classic Resin Strap Digital Sport Watch]], a truly low-end classic retro, to find out if I would even wear a watch after 10 years without one. I loved it, and as a time piece it is a bargain. But I wanted something bigger and chunkier.

I really like the look of this 3500, the 3000, and the 2500. They have slightly different features, and it may be that you would get more use out of a tide graph over say sunrise/sunset times, in which case you are going to click over to the 2500 page. I decided I would buy whichever one came up for a great price, and it turned out to be this watch. If price is your thing, you need to add a plugin called keepa to your Chrome, as it tracks and charts the price of just about everything on Amazon. High-margin articles such as watches have very volatile prices, and if you can wait for a sale, you can save a lot of money.

So I got a very good price, and I'm happy with the purchase, although the vendor sold me one that had been opened, perhaps as an in-store demo. However, there were no marks on it at all, no fingerprints etc, so I decided to run with it.

It is fairly bulky, it is more than snug under close fitting sleeves, and makes it harder to remove Parkers and other winter clothing. Not a complaint, just an observation. The thick metal rim provides a lot of protection for the mineral glass and looks good. This ring has no detents and is too stiff to turn with one finger. The deeply recessed glass means that to scratch it, you need to whack it on a really awkward corner.

The display may look similar to, say, the PRG-270, but in fact the numbers are noticeably bigger with more LCD area. So if you step down to a 270, you lose not just the radio control but a small amount of readability. The backlight is pale blue, lit by two LEDs on the left, but evenly lit across the face with no problem reading any part of the face. It has an auto-light when it detects you tilting your wrist - this triggers all the time, even when driving. I will turn this off for long drives at night (or just remove the watch).

I leave the display switched to the barometer chart. It gives me a good idea what is going on with the weather. It has a long recording time, 48 hours I think, which is more than you really need. A 24 hour chart would be fine. Note that if you drive to work, with an altitude change, it is going to mess up your nice chart (I work from home).

Which brings me to sensors. They appear to be very accurate. I calibrated the baro to the sea-level value for my location, using the website of the local university. Comparing the baro charts side by side, this watch is very accurate. The thermometer is fine, but obviously it records the temperature close to your wrist (usually in the range 75-85 degrees) and not ambient. If you google up a Stevenson Screen, you'll see that to reliably get air temperature, you need a specially constructed and vented box of around 1 cubic foot, double-roofed etc. So no complaints about the thermometer, please. The compass works fine, I would not navigate across open water with it, though.

The radio receiver works for me about 2 days out of 3. I am a bit over 1k miles from Colorado. The instructions say to put the watch near a window that faces Colorado, etc, but I think solar winds and other overnight electrical/atmospheric noise make more difference. My watch gains about 2 seconds a week when running free, which is not nearly as good as my ten dollar F-91W (2 seconds a month).

My watch struggles to stay on "H" (high) charge during the winter. The great advantage of solar watches is not that they run for ever (they wont) but that you can use energy sucking ancillaries like the sensors and the backlight without worry. My circumstances are, a dimly lit room during the day, and the sun is gone by the time I get outside. So the watch gradually discharges during the week and recovers at the weekend. This is fine, and is the reason why it has a power saving mode (turns off the display and eventually almost everything else during periods of low charge). The power saving mode is switchable but I can think of no reason not to use it. I would guess that the battery lasts as long as a regular battery in a regular watch with none of the goodies i.e. 7-10 years.

It is waterproof to so many feet. I would take this with a grain of salt - google up wrist watch on wikipedia, the published depth ratings may not be what you expect. I am sure this watch is safe in the pool or in the shower, but why risk it? I take mine off.

Overall I am very happy with this watch. I would not pay list price for it, not because it is not worth it but because you don't have to. If you are a Casio guy, and want a full-featured watch with all the bells and whistles, put this on your list.
rating 4.0. Four Stars good watch, but it is too big if you have a small wrist like myself

rating 5.0. This One's Special! Well now!!! This one's something special, isn't it? A little nicer than the rest.
Of all the Casio's I own, this PRW-3500 is my favorite.
NOTE: This watch's LCD display is MUCH clearer and brighter than the Amazon image(s) show! See other web images, it's very impressive.
For comparison, I currently own:

PRG-270-1 (black, some orange print)
PRG-270D-7CR (titanium band)
PRW-3000-2BCR (blue/purple)
GW-9400-1CR Rangeman
WV-300 Wave Ceptor (discontinued)
And now, the PRW-3500-1CR

All of the new Casio sensor v3.0 watches have come a long way in accuracy. Where I couldn't accurately calibrate the earlier Pathfinders, I can now calibrate everything and get very accurate readings with the new Pro Trek series. Here are some comments on the functions:

COMPASS: Very accurate, works as good as my mechanical compasses. The movable bezel on this model is redundant, but still very useful and looks classy. Note: all magnetic compasses are negatively affected by magnetism, metal objects, etc. Best not to be used in the car, near electrical equipment or audio speakers which have powerful magnets. Best out in the field - and always calibrate right before using.

BAROMETER: Accurate to within .05 inHg. More accurate than than my iPhone Weather Channel App, because it's real-time, whereas with the phone app you have to wait for periodic updates plus there may be a discrepancy due to the Weather Channels' sensor location(s). The barometer's Weather Alert mode works great on all of my Pro Trek's. Activate it when you know foul weather is coming over the next 24 hrs, and it will alert you when storms are approaching. Also good for camping & hiking - you can activate it to be alerted about any sudden storm approaching so that you have enough time to find cover.

ALTIMETER: Affected by air pressure, so accuracy is more general, although the reading intervals have been improved. Best for hiking than car rides because snapshot intervals aren't suited for fast movement across terrain, they're suited for walking and cycling. Also, if you're hiking and a storm is approaching, your readings will not be accurate because changes to the barometric pressure will affect the altimeter readings. best use in stable weather. That said, it truly is the best technology available short of a GPS device.

TEMPERATURE: Very accurate. I have all of my Pro Trek's calibrated using a small electronic temperature gauge, and all of them read exactly the same as my wall-mounted digital temperature gauge (when located at the same location as the watches). You have to take the watch off to get accurate readings, and this will probably never change due to the proximity of the sensor to your body. The sensors are very sensitive - simply picking up the watch will effect the reading. I recommend keeping it off for 30 minutes (not 10, 15 or even 20) to give the watch enough time to fully acclimate. If temperate is important to you, clip the watch to your backpack or tent. Do not leave it in the sun.

BACKLIGHT: Works great. I love the brighter illumination of the new LED lights. In pitch dark you may be able to find items in your tent. I am very happy that Casio changed the afterglow limit from 1 second to 3 seconds. A one-second backlight limit (Pathfinder series) on any watch is an immediate deal breaker for me. I love the backlight's Auto-On feature: I wear one to bed and just flick my wrist to see the time whenever I wake in the middle of the night. I also love the location and knurled feel of the backight button - easy to feel in the dark.

BATTERY: I've been using a couple of these models for a while now and have yet to see a fully charged battery reach Medium from normal use. I suppose you can run it down pretty quickly if you keep using the backlight too much. When they're new I'll leave them on a sunny window sill all day for the first couple of days for maximum charge. After that I regularly put them in a window sill once or twice a week for several hours just to keep them topped them off.

ATOMIC TIMEKEEPING: Reception depends on your area and current weather conditions. This function only works at night, (usually 4am for me), even when wearing the watch. It doesn't work well for me on cloudy or bad weather nights, but you don't need to calibrate more often than once a week even if you're particular about accuracy. Don't expect to be able to calibrate 24 hrs a day unless you live very close to the radio transmission. I've got an Atomic Casio from 10 years ago and it has always synchronized, usually without me having to place it in a window. Keep it away from magnetic items like audio speakers.

ALARMS: The 5 alarms are cool to have, but there's no way to label them to distinguish which alarm is for what purpose. I love the Snooze alarm - however, I do wish it had the ability to choose specific days of the week, such as only M-F (exclude weekends). So it's either all or nothing with the alarms - you'll have to remember to turn them off when you don't need them to be on. The alarms on the Pro Trek's are about as loud as they get with Casio watches. Just so you know, if these aren't loud enough for you, don't bother looking at other digital watches. I also have the Casio G-Shock Rangeman (much more expensive than the Pro Treks) and its alarms aren't even as loud as the Pro Treks (that's not to discredit the Rangemen, it's an incredible timepiece!). So in any case, if you're going to wear your digital watch covered by the sleeve of your Gor-Tex down-lined wilderness coat in 20 MPH winds - it's not likely you're going to hear the alarms. And I doubt you'll hear them in your nylon tent flapping in the winds at the top of that mountain peak either. It's just not gonna happen. In fairness, I know of no other digital watches that are louder. And for those who think a vibration feature is the answer, imagine the cost, size and need for more battery power being a major obstacle.

SUNRISE/SUNSET: Once I properly calibrated for the LAT/LONG of my exact location, this feature is now in perfect sync with The Weather Channel app on my iPhone. (You'll have to find the lat/long website calculator for your area, Amazon won't allow me to post the link.)

Other functions such as the stopwatch, countdown timer, world time, hourly chime, etc. are all standard features, and work as well as you would expect. v3.0 ProTrek watches are as accurate as the technology gets without GPS, but here's the best thing: they are the simplest effective gadgets to use! No batteries to change, no need to ever set the time and date again, and the user-interface is intuitive (after a quick read through the booklet). And the learning curve, if there even is one, is low and even fun. The instructions are great, and provide lots of important tips for best use.

ONLY ONE CON: The band is very difficult to remove on my PRG-270-1, and this one is even more difficult due to the hole in the rubber strap retainer. It's very frustrating trying to get the strap to slide out from under that retainer. The best way I've found so far is you have to be able to use one hand to both pull the strap out while also squeezing the rubber retainer enough to get the strap's hump out from under it. This would make more sense if you own the watch and can see it visually. But this is by no means a deal breaker, as long as I can get the watch off without breaking it, I'm OK.

Highly recommended!!
I'm very excited to own this beautifully made electronic timepiece.
Casio has come a long way.
Don't hesitate to buy one!

rating 4.0. Love the watch - hate the strap! Love the watch - hate the strap... Like almost all 'buckle-type' straps, it is very difficult to get a comfortable fit. The watch is either flopping around on the wrist, or else cutting off the blood flow. Also, the little hump on the end of the male part of the strap locks the strap against the holding 'hoop', making it extremely difficult to get the watch off. Just this afternoon took the watch to my 'watch guy' and had the strap replaced with a Velcro band. It was either that, or else return it, since I couldn't wear it with the supplied strap. Works well on the Velcro strap.

I obviously haven't been wearing the watch a lot, but I have played around with it quite a bit. Found a place where I can put it where it keeps the charge up - took about 3 hours full sun to get the charge indicator from M to H - and also it detects the atomic signal. During the Summer, I wear a watch all the time with short sleeves, so charging will not be an issue. Just now, however, I am still wearing long sleeves, so the watch doesn't get sunshine all day.

This is not a simple device, but the basic operations are fairly straight-forward. I will need to sit down again with the manual, and play with the 'abc' functions, but I think I have everything else straight.

What to say...? I like a big, meaty watch, and that is definitely what this is. If you have a slim wrist, this may be a bit overpowering... Very easy to read in most lighting conditions. I was a bit concerned that it looked a bit dark in the photos, but in the flesh it is fine. The 'auto-lighting' function is a bit of a waste. I thought it was a great idea, but almost every time you move your arm, the light comes on. It would be nice if it would detect just a nice 'flick' of the wrist, but failing that, you need to push the button. The light does work well, having done that.

I have several watches, and my daily choice is going to be between this one, and my tritium-illuminated 'tactical' watch. This is my first trek-style acquisition, and it is filling a nice slot in my collection.

rating 2.0. Great watch but missing a key function I need. This an excellent watch. However, one of the main features I use is the count down timer for yacht racing. There is no "progress beeper" on this watch. It only beeps when the total time is up; there are no intermediate beeps. My old Casio PRG-70 -- one of the first ABC watches -- finally failed; it beeped at 10, 5, 4, 3, 2, and 1 minutes, followed by 50, 40, 30,20,10, 5, 4,3,2,1, and 0 seconds. This sequence was excellent for adjusting your boat speed to be at the starting line at top speed when the gun fired.

I returned the PRW-3500 for a PRW-2500 which is an earlier version of the PRW-3500 but without the improved sensors. It does have a progress beeper, but the beep sequence is not as detailed in the final one minute. It will have to do.

Why do Casio engineers and programmers have to keep "improving" on things that were tailored to meet specific sport requirements. The PRG-70
was sold, among other things, as a yachtsman's watch.

It IS small, but.... ...I happen to have a third piercing that falls just even with the lower part of the thick cartiledge of my ear I think it's called the tragus - so this little dragon does fit me.  I knew it was for the left ear where I have that higher piercing so for the price gave it a shot.  It's prettier than in the picture (looks more gold) and really is lovely.  It does not fit perfectly over the top of my ears(I am 47 years old - I understand our ears keep growing with age - lol), however I normally wear headscarves that just wrap the top of my ears.  You don't see the wing that way, but his head pokes out.  My other thought would be to try some eyelash adhesive to keep it from slipping off if I wasn't wearing a scarf.  Otherwise it is not too heavy and almost don't feel I am wearing it.  Finally it isn't quite anatomically correct as it pulls the top of the ear forward, but I couldn't bear to give it four stars as I personally love it and will wear it.  Shipping was also fairly fast.  Will purchase from this seller again.

rating 5.0. PERFECT for me, but I don't intend to use them as scarves... These are VERY sheer scarves with a little bit of shine or "sparkle" to them, I say "sparkle" with quotes because there aren't any sparkle specks or anything, whatever its make of just refracts light. Now, like I said in the headline, these are perfect for me, but I have no intention of ever using them as scarves. If you held one end of ALL TWELVE scarves in one hand, it would be equal to the thickness of a good quality pashmina... One of the photos I took, you can make out every detail of my daughter's face and outfit THROUGH the black one... They're THAT thin. They're not hemmed, but they wont fray or run unless you try tell hard to make them do it.

Now, you're probably asking yourself "so what's she going to use them for?" The answer has many different forms. Jewish women call them tichels, women - like myself - who are not Jewish and/or haven't mastered their pronunciation of "ch" call them headwraps. These "scarves" will be perfect for keeping my hair wrapped over the summer without sweating to death haha

rating 3.0. Scarves Not Scarves This set is as pictured; twelve great colors.  However, these ARE NOT scarves.  Each is a piece cut from a larger supply of crinkled nylon (?) of a slightly heavier gauge than nylon stockings.  They are not hemmed or finished in any way, and I found that many of them are fraying at the ends.  It was my thought to use two or more together since they are so sheer.  If you treat these delicately, you may not have too much trouble with fraying, but they will not last very long if used regularly.  I think I will always have to check for fraying ends before wearing these, but the colors are really very nice.  Had I known they were unfinished, I would not have made the purchase, as $12.99 is way more than these flimsy "scarves" are worth.

rating 4.0. SUPER fabulous and cute!!! I LOVE ridiculously dramatic, big sunglasses. It may be silly, but I feel super glamorous in them, and wanted to get a new, ridiculously big pair to take with me on vacation (to match my ridiculously big floppy beach hat). These glasses only cost me $7.20, and I love a bargain. They look SO good on. I have a round-ish shaped face, and I found these to be flattering. They did a good job of keeping the sun out of my eyes on the beach and in the car. I will admit, I can tell why they were so cheap, the gold squares on the stems of the glasses, that is rubbing off and almost completely gone after only 2 weeks of wearing. They scratch pretty easy, but they are still wearable and look so cute, that I don't mind. If you're looking for inexpensive, super glamorous, ridiculously big deal sunglass look, then I'd recommend these!

rating 5.0. Bug eyed and loving it. These are my favorite oversized shades. It is so hard to find glasses with lenses this large in retail stores anymore, so I was super excited to see that I could still find them, and for cheap! I ended up breaking my first pair by sitting on them, but I knew I could affordably replace them so I wasn't too upset. They are really comfortable to wear, the only time I have ever experienced any sort of discomfort is after wearing them for excessively long periods of time (like a road trip or beach day without ever removing them) and I will get a sore spot on the bridge of my nose, but nothing severe. I am sure people think I am ridiculous with such big glasses on, but I absolutely love the coverage. Also, they're great at holding my hair back once I put them on my head indoors as well.

rating 5.0. Unbeatable Value and Soo Beautiful!! I bought these earrings along with the sterling silver pair. I gave the gold pair as a gift and kept the silver pair for myself. Now where else can you buy this type of item for a .01? I mean really?? Ok, so the shipping is maybe a little high, but even at that, you couldn't buy the same for even at that total! I do admit, that the stones are larger than I was actually wanting, only because I have small ear lobes, so I will order the multi-sizes option later. With all that being said, these earrings are GORGEOUS!! They sparkle and look so much like the 'real thing'!! So, yes, I am very happy with my purchase and Amazon always delivers on their promise, I received the order just like they said I would and will continue to shop with them- keep up the good work Amazon team!

rating 5.0. Perfect Ok. For the amount of money I spent on these earrings, these earrings are great! They came in a little velvet pouch and were just as described.

I've worn them a week straight now and so far no peeling or color change. The cz stone is shiny and clear--not cloudy. Definately well worth the money!

rating 5.0. Beautiful earrings, unbeatable price! Granted, I probably got these earrings on sale. I've never spent less on a piece of jewelry! One penny! There were shipping chargers of course, but they were reasonable. Shipping was fast and they arrived whole and safely.

The earrings themselves are beautiful, full of fire and sparkle, nicely set in the stud to afford the most view of the stone. The gold plating on the stud is rich and yellow, just what I wanted! The back attaches securely and fits tight over the prong. This appears to be a quality crafted piece and I think they will last a long, long time.

I plan on wearing these in my wedding this month, but they are not too gaudy or over-the-top for everyday wear, either. In fact, I might wear them to work today!

input: rating 5.0. but the prices for these necklaces was fantastic, even with the addition of a shipping charge I purchased a few necklaces from Qiyun. There was a shipping charge of $2.99/item, but the prices for these necklaces was fantastic, even with the addition of a shipping charge.  I was quoted in the order that they would arrive between April 22 to May 8. I was so pleasantly surprised to open my mailbox today, April 9th and have a package full of necklaces. Everything arrived in wonderful condition and looked just like the pics.  I wore this one to work today and had so many comments on how pretty it looked. I can't wait to wear the others. Thank you.
